title: FileLaunch Module
input: file_path: string, options?: { alias_resolution?: boolean, fallback_handler?: string }
hint: When given a file path or reference, resolve it to a concrete file, determine its type, find or infer the appropriate application to open it, launch it, and report outcome. Should handle apps, documents, aliases, and symlinks. Must not require user to specify the target application.

title: Arithmetic Drill Module
input: Develop a bounded, local-first arithmetic drill module for children ages 5-8. The module presents short, timed or untimed arithmetic questions (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division) and accepts numeric input. Provide immediate visual and audio feedback on correctness, track session score and streak, and offer simple celebratory micro-rewards (sounds, animations, stickers) after correct answers or milestones. Include a mode selector to choose operation type and difficulty range. No cloud sync, no accounts, no ads. Prioritize clear, large touch targets and child-friendly UI.
hint: local-only arithmetic drill module for kids ages 5-8, focused on speed and encouragement, mode selection for operation type, immediate feedback, streak tracking, minimal UI, offline-capable, no cloud or account needed, parent/teacher configuration for difficulty range

title: Personal Physical Music Collection Catalog
input: Build a local-first Personal Music Collection Catalog module in Python using SQLite that allows users to add, edit, delete, and search physical music items (CDs, vinyl, cassettes). Each item must include: artist, album title, format type, condition rating, purchase date, purchase price, vendor/source, and nested track listing. Include full-text search across all fields, filtering by format/artist/year/condition, CSV import/export, and a simple CLI or GUI for browsing the collection. The module should be self-contained with no cloud dependencies.
hint: Focus on bounded local functionality - no API integrations, no cloud sync, no social features. Just the cataloguing workflow with essential metadata capture and retrieval.

title: Click-to-Call Contact Dialer
input: Create a click-to-call contact dialer that stores contacts locally, detects phone numbers on web pages, and initiates outbound calls via connected softphone with a single click
hint: Focus on browser extension architecture, tel: link handling, auto-detection of 7-15 digit phone numbers, and whitelist-based site rules - not a full CRM

title: NYC Subway Station Navigator
input: User wants to browse the NYC subway network station-by-station and trace connections manually to plan a route
hint: interactive navigator module with offline-capable station data, clickable line/connection buttons, and optional journey recording

title: Color Spark Quiz
input: preschool color quiz multiple choice children 3-6 identify colors
hint: Bounded quiz module - do not expand into general learning management or multi-subject platform. Focus on color recognition only.

title: Archimedes Polygon Approximation Explorer
input: A learner wants to understand how Archimedes approximated pi using polygons, without reading a textbook. They need to see a circle with overlaid inscribed/circumscribed polygons, adjust the side count, and watch the perimeter converge toward circumference.
hint: Make the polygon dynamic - user drags a slider to increase sides from 3 to 96+. Show both inscribed and circumscribed polygons simultaneously. Display real-time perimeter values and the error margin from true pi. Include a brief historical context panel. Keep it to one focused screen.

title: Personal Citation Manager
input: I need a personal citation reference manager that operates locally without cloud services. It should allow manual entry of citation records with smart field navigation, support batch import from standard formats (BibTeX, RIS, MEDLINE/SilverPlatter), and export citations in multiple formats. Include a bibliography generator that formats references according to common style templates and a print-friendly view for producing formatted reference lists. Keep scope bounded to individual personal use, not team collaboration.
hint: Focus on local-first sovereignty, batch import robustness, and clean export rather than extensive style customization or cloud sync

title: Font Specimen Generator
input: list of installed system fonts, desired specimen sizes, sample text string, page layout preferences
hint: Generate a printable font specimen sheet that displays each selected font in its own typeface, rendering full paragraphs at multiple sizes for design review and kerning assessment

title: DictCheck Local
input: You are DictCheck Local, a bounded local-first spell-check module. Given plain text input and optional custom dictionary file paths, scan the text against a system dictionary and any user-provided custom dictionaries to identify misspellings. Return a structured list of suspected misspellings with their positions, suggested corrections from the merged dictionary, and a flag for words that may be proper nouns or domain terms suitable for addition to a custom dictionary. Do not connect to external APIs or remote services. Operate entirely offline with local dictionary files.
hint: Input: raw text string + optional array of custom dictionary file paths (one word per line). Output: JSON array of { word, startOffset, endOffset, suggestions[], isProperNounCandidate }. System dictionary built-in; custom dicts merged at runtime.

title: Tab Practice Player
input: A desktop application that loads guitar tablature files (GuitarPro formats, ASCII tab), parses the notation and timing data, and plays back the tablature with adjustable tempo, looping capabilities for sections, and navigation controls. The application should allow users to slow down playback for practice, loop specific measures or sections, and visually follow along with the scrolling tablature.
hint: Create a bounded desktop application for loading and playing guitar tablature files with tempo control, section looping, and measure navigation for practice purposes.

title: Kappa Calculator for Binary Inter-Rater Agreement
input: I need a standalone calculator that takes a 2x2 contingency table (a,b,c,d) where a=both raters agree yes, b=rater A yes/rater B no, c=rater A no/rater B yes, d=both raters agree no. Calculate Cohen's Kappa = (observed agreement - chance agreement) / (1 - chance agreement) where observed agreement = (a+d)/total and chance agreement = ((a+b)(a+c) + (c+d)(b+d)) / total^2. Display the Kappa value rounded to 3 decimals and provide a text interpretation using the standard scale: <0 = poor, 0.01-0.20 = slight, 0.21-0.40 = fair, 0.41-0.60 = moderate, 0.61-0.80 = substantial, 0.81-1.00 = almost perfect. Include a brief note showing the formula or the calculation steps. No data storage, no external API calls, no login. Just input cells, calculate button, and result.
hint: A clean, minimal web tool or desktop widget where a user enters the four counts from a 2x2 agreement matrix and gets an instant Cohen's Kappa score with plain-language interpretation. No login, no data export required. Just input-compute-interpret.

title: Condition Reference Module
input: Write a bounded, condition-specific patient education reference module for local-first use. The module should cover prevention guidance, symptom reference, diagnosis information, treatment options, and regional context. Structure it as a navigable knowledge base without requiring network connectivity or user accounts. Prioritize clear, action-oriented sections that patients or caregivers can reference independently.
hint: A local-first, bounded module that serves as a single-condition reference for patients or caregivers. It should allow non-linear navigation across prevention, symptoms, diagnosis, treatment, and local/regional context. No user accounts, no SaaS features—just a navigable reference structured around the condition's natural information architecture.

title: Lucrece Scholar Reader
input: Input the text of The Rape of Lucrece to be annotated
hint: Use the annotation layer to attach marginal notes, highlight passages of significance, and link to the study guide panel for contextual commentary

title: In-Context Color Utility Panel
input: You are a bounded color utility panel designed to live embedded within visual builder and editor applications. Your sole purpose is to enable rapid color selection, adjustment, and application without forcing the user to leave their authoring context. You provide: 1. **Swatch Grid** - A compact display of current palette colors organized for one-click selection 2. **Quick Picker** - An integrated color picker with hue/saturation/lightness or RGB/HEX input 3. **Palette Management** - Load, save, and switch between named color palettes 4. **Recent Colors** - Auto-tracked history of recently applied colors for fast re-use 5. **Apply Actions** - Context-aware buttons to apply the selected color to the currently focused element, layer, or property You are NOT a standalone color theory tool, design software, or palette generator. You exist only to serve the in-context editing workflow. When the user selects a color or palette, you should immediately reflect that choice and provide clear apply mechanisms. You stay out of the way when not actively being used.
hint: Frame the bounded module as a UI component or embeddable panel that visual builder tools would include. Use component-based framing - think 'ColorUtilityPanel as a docked/floating widget' rather than 'ColorTool as a full application'. The scope is one specific utility panel, not a color management system.

title: Live Recording Archive Manager
input: archival audio recordings metadata database local-first sovereign personal collection manager live music archive
hint: bounded cataloging tool for personal or institutional audio archive

title: AudioChannelMixer
input: Build a micro audio engine module for applications that provides playback of sound assets with independent per-channel volume (amplitude) control during playback and output routing to mono, left, right, or stereo modes. The module should be embeddable as a library/plugin and include reference examples for common application frameworks.
hint: Focus on: (1) low-latency playback, (2) real-time per-channel amplitude adjustment while playing, (3) output mode switching without interruption, (4) cross-platform support (Windows/Mac/Linux), (5) simple API surface suitable as a plugin/XCMD replacement

title: Truncate XFCN 1.1
input: Build a bounded modern UI text-overflow truncation utility for labels, lists, and table cells for my own node. It should help me Fit variable-length text into constrained interface space while preserving readability with ellipsis and use a clear input text plus layout constraints (width, font, size) producing display-safe truncated text with ellipsis workflow shaped around shave, truncate, text, ellipsize. Recover one concrete module, not a broad product suite. Search evidence may refine modern wording and structure, but it must not replace the retained row's core user job.
hint: Stay close to the retained card job, keep the MSS shape recoverable, and avoid copying handcrafted wording while using current workflow vocabulary such as shave, truncate, text, ellipsize. Prefer a sovereign/local-first module over a generic cloud or team product. If the original medium is obsolete, preserve the durable user job rather than copying nostalgia or over-generalizing into a vague organizer. Search should only sharpen the module shape or modern vocabulary; if search is noisy or generic, fall back to the retained row rather than inventing a broader product.

title: CityGrid Navigator Module
input: user needs to look up how to navigate a city using transit options and understand street-number orientation
hint: focus on lookup/read-only reference, not real-time transit tracking or route planning

title: RegionCodeValidator - State/Province and Area Code Cross-Validation Utility
input: I need a module that validates US state names against their 2-letter postal codes and validates that phone area codes match the state. The module should support bidirectional lookups (state name ↔ postal code, area code ↔ state).
hint: Reference the retention row: original XFCN 'cbAreaCode()' converted to validate area code ↔ state mappings, 'cbPostAbbrev()' converted to validate state name ↔ postal abbreviation. Use lookup tables rather than external APIs for local-first operation.

title: Personal Skills Self-Assessment Intake Module
input: user provides responses to structured questionnaire across communication, empathy, assertiveness, emotional regulation, and interpersonal skills categories
hint: workflow is intake-first: collect responses, compute scores silently, present profile without requiring counselor presentee

title: Couch Potato Handbook (Personal Movie Catalog Module)
input: Personal movie collection tracker - local database, offline-first, structured movie reference with metadata fields (title, actors, critic rating, MPAA rating, description), personal collection list with owned/watched/wishlist status, simple search and browse, metadata import from online sources, no cloud dependency, no social features, no streaming discovery, user-owned data only.
hint: Small desktop app - SQLite backend, metadata sync optional, collection export, not a SaaS

title: Script Event Inspector
input: I need to debug why my automation script is failing at a specific step. I want to capture all script events/messages in real-time, watch them execute with the ability to slow down or pause execution, and review a persistent log after the run completes.
hint: The prompt should request a bounded tool/module for intercepting and visualizing script execution events with playback controls and logging

title: Klingon Bilingual Dictionary with pIqaD Converter
input: You are building a Klingon bilingual dictionary and transliteration module called "pIqaD Lookup". The module lets users: 1) Search for Klingon terms by Latin transliteration or English definition; 2) View detailed entries with part of speech, definition, usage examples, and cultural notes; 3) Convert text bidirectionally between Okrandian transliteration, xifan hol scheme, and pIqaD script (using Unicode Private Use Area mapping). Include a standalone conversion tool for quick transliteration without a dictionary lookup. Keep it bounded: no language learning exercises, no community features, no broader Star Trek reference suite. Focus purely on lookup and conversion.
hint: Build a bounded reference module: user enters a term or phrase in either Klingon Latin transliteration (Okrand or xifan hol) or pIqaD script, and the module returns the bilingual definition along with a conversion to the alternate orthography. Include search-by-def, reverse lookup, and a conversion utility for standalone transliteration tasks.

title: Student Discipline Referral Module
input: School or district needs discipline tracking without full LMS overhead
hint: Generate a local-first discipline referral module with entry form, action tracking, parent communication log, and conference report export. Include student record linking and status workflow.

title: Personal Art Collection Browser
input: A personal collection of 47 sea-life digital paintings and sketches created between 1989-1992 using Fractal Design Sketcher and Wacom ArtZ tablet, currently stored as separate image files with original filenames; user wants to browse by theme, tag with descriptive keywords like 'dolphin' 'crab' 'tropical' 'grayscale', and quickly find artworks by keyword search
hint: Local-first sovereign module for managing personal digital art collections with manual keyword tagging and instant search, no cloud or social features, focus on privacy and offline access

title: PopModel Workbook
input: Design a single bounded interactive population scenario modeler application that: (1) loads demographic, environmental, and economic data for countries from a local dataset, (2) presents input forms for adjusting fertility rates, mortality rates, migration numbers, and economic indicators per country, (3) runs cohort-component or similar projection calculations to generate future population scenarios, (4) displays results as tables and charts showing population projections under different assumption sets, (5) includes a glossary of demographic terms and contextual help suitable for classroom use, (6) supports exporting projection results to standard formats like CSV or JSON for further analysis. The application should be local-first, privacy-preserving, and suitable for educational environments without requiring network connectivity.
hint: A bounded desktop application for interactive population projection modeling at the country level, allowing users to adjust demographic, environmental, and economic indicators and visualize resulting projections with export capabilities for educational and analytical use

title: Portfolio Binding Workshop
input: I am a photographer/artist who needs to create a professional physical presentation portfolio for showing my work to clients or galleries. I want to make it myself using Japanese bookbinding techniques because I want something elegant, affordable, and customizable.
hint: This should be a bounded DIY workflow that walks through the specific process of assembling a presentation portfolio using Japanese stab binding or similar techniques. Include materials sourcing, step-by-step assembly, design choices for covers and paper, and quality check points.

title: UI Asset Studio Module
input: Create a bounded local-first module called 'UI Asset Studio' that provides a curated catalog of reusable interface assets (icon set, button templates, sound effects, custom fonts) with built-in utilities for copying assets to clipboard, importing into projects, inspecting resource metadata, and organizing favorites. The module should function as a sovereign tool that developers can use standalone to speed up UI prototyping and development without requiring cloud connectivity or broad platform dependencies.
hint: Design a bounded module for managing and distributing reusable UI assets (icons, buttons, sounds, fonts) to app developers with quick copy/import workflows

title: Invoice Composer Module
input: user provides: client name, line items (description, quantity, unit price), tax rate, payment terms, notes
hint: You are a local-first invoice composition module. Create invoices with line items, apply configured tax rates, generate printable PDF/HTML output, and maintain a searchable local archive of issued invoices. Operate entirely client-side with user-controlled data storage.

title: FrameForge - Minimal Animation Bootstrapping Tool
input: You need a lightweight animation editor that creates a new animation project from a template. The editor should have a canvas for drawing frames, a timeline strip for navigating frames, basic drawing tools (pencil, eraser), playback controls, and export to GIF or webM. The module should work offline in a single HTML file or minimal folder structure.
hint: Focus on bootstrap workflow - create new animation from template, add/delete frames, draw on canvas, preview playback, export. Keep it minimal and local-first.

title: Personal Music Collection Catalog
input: I want to catalog my personal music collection including vinyl records, CDs, and digital files. I need to add items manually or via barcode/search, enrich entries with album art and metadata from an online music database, search and filter my collection by artist/album/genre/year/format, track item condition and purchase details, and generate inventory reports.
hint: A bounded, local-first module focused purely on collection inventory and management - not playback, streaming, or social features. The user owns their data and it's searchable for collection lookup and purchase decisions.

title: Archival Media Label Designer
input: I need to design and print custom adhesive labels for my VHS tape collection and archival media storage boxes.
hint: Focus on die-cut template accuracy for specific media dimensions (VHS cassette: 190×25×104mm cardboard slipcase, 201×27×120mm plastic clamshell) rather than generic label creation.

title: VerseFragment - Local Haiku Composer
input: Write a bounded local-first haiku generator module called 'VerseFragment'. It generates 5-7-5 syllable haikus using user-provided vocabulary lists. Include editable word banks with syllable counts, a generator that composes random combinations respecting the 5-7-5 structure, and iterative output history. The module runs entirely client-side with no external API dependencies. Prioritize privacy, minimal load time, and the whimsical creative spirit of the original HyperCard stack.
hint: This is a creative text generator with vocabulary controls, not a general-purpose AI writing tool.

title: California Reference Module
input: Create a bounded reference module for looking up structured facts about California: cities, counties, geographic features, notable organizations, demographics, and points of interest. Include category-based navigation and entity pages with linked references.
hint: Focus on structured factual lookup: cities, counties, landmarks, organizations, demographics. Do not include real-time data feeds or government services.

title: Real-Time Inline Field Validator
input: Create a bounded TypeScript/JavaScript module for real-time inline field validation that: 1) Accepts validation rule definitions per field, 2) Triggers validation on input/blur events, 3) Manages visual feedback states (valid/invalid/pending), 4) Supports custom rule extension and async validation for edge cases, 5) Optionally applies input masks for structured fields like phone numbers or dates. The module should be framework-agnostic, tree-shakeable, and export simple hooks or utility functions for integration into data-entry forms. Do not include full form state management or backend submission logic.
hint: Design a lightweight, composable client-side field validation module that validates input as the user types, provides instant visual feedback (valid/invalid states), supports custom validation rules and input masking, and integrates cleanly into form workflows without requiring server calls for feedback. Include patterns for common field types (email, phone, numeric ranges) and extensible rule registration.

title: Solar Terminator Viewer
input: current UTC timestamp, optional city search query, optional date for seasonal preview
hint: Output: rendered map with day/night boundary polygon, city markers, and local time overlays. Single bounded tool—no scheduling, no calendar integration, no communications features.

title: Baseball Contact Directory
input: design a baseball contact directory with entity types team player association search filters bulk import export stale record tracking
hint: Create a module that lets users add, search, filter, and manage contact records for baseball teams, players, coaches, and associations. Include entity type, organization, address, phone, email, notes, and last-verified date. Support bulk import/export and stale record flagging. Keep it lightweight and local-first.

title: Bounded Carousel Navigator
input: I need a bounded carousel navigator component that allows users to horizontally navigate through a limited collection of items using a direct manipulation slider/scrollbar. The component should: 1) Maintain current index position in an ordered collection (bounded, known count), 2) Provide a horizontal slider/thumb control that users can drag to jump to any position, 3) Support step-based navigation (next/previous) via controls, 4) Display the current item in a viewport, 5) Handle boundary conditions (disable prev at first item, disable next at last item). This is a standalone local-first component, not a full carousel library.
hint: The bounded nature is key - it's for navigating a finite, known set rather than an infinite scroll. Direct manipulation via slider distinguishes this from button-only navigation.

title: DataFormatter Utility Module
input: Design a bounded data formatting utility module that provides consistent date/time parsing and formatting, number formatting with locale support, and string normalization functions. The module should be stateless and expose clear, reusable functions for normalizing and formatting dates, numbers, and strings across different workflows. Include support for common format patterns and locale-aware conversions.
hint: Create a bounded, local-first utility module with stateless helper functions for date/time parsing, number formatting, and string normalization. Keep it focused and single-purpose rather than a broad library.

title: Currency Converter Widget
input: A user needs to convert a specific amount from one currency to another using current exchange rates. They provide: amount, source currency, target currency. The system returns: converted amount, applied exchange rate, rate source URL, last-updated timestamp. The conversion is read-only calculation - no transaction, no account required.
hint: Focus on the conversion calculation workflow only. Do not expand into rate alerts, historical charts, multi-currency invoicing, or wallet features. The module is a bounded utility for on-demand amount conversion, not a financial planning suite.

title: FractionPrime Calculator
input: >>> |>_USER_REQUEST_<<< :: I need a bounded local-first desktop math utility that combines fraction arithmetic with prime number operations. It should handle: (1) fraction operations (add/subtract/multiply/divide two fractions, simplify results), (2) GCD and LCM calculations for two integers, (3) primality checking for a single number, (4) prime factorization of any integer up to 10 trillion, and (5) generating primes within a given range or count. The UI should have mode selection, numeric input fields, operation controls, and clear result display. All computation must run entirely client-side with no network dependency. Write the SPEC.md and then implement the complete application. |>_SPECIFICATION_<<< :: # FractionPrime Calculator - Specification ## 1. Project Overview - **Project Name**: FractionPrime Calculator - **Type**: Desktop utility application (local-first) - **Core Functionality**: Combined fraction arithmetic and prime number theory calculator - **Target Users**: Students, educators, developers needing quick math computations offline ## 2. UI/UX Specification ### Layout Structure - Single window application (800x600 default, resizable min 600x500) - Header: app title and mode tabs - Main content: input area + operation controls + results - Footer: version info, optional history toggle ### Visual Design - **Color Palette**: - Background: #1a1a2e (dark navy) - Surface: #16213e (slightly lighter) - Primary accent: #0f3460 (deep blue) - Action: #e94560 (coral red) - Text primary: #eaeaea - Text secondary: #a0a0a0 - Success: #4ecca3 (mint green) - **Typography**: - Font: "JetBrains Mono", "Consolas", monospace - Heading: 18px bold - Body: 14px regular - Numbers/Results: 16px medium - **Spacing**: 8px base unit; 16px component gaps - **Visual Effects**: subtle box-shadow on cards, smooth tab transitions ### Components - **Mode Tabs**: Fractions | GCD-LCM | Prime Check | Factorize | Generate - **Input Fields**: Labeled numeric inputs with validation - **Operation Buttons**: Primary action buttons per mode - **Results Panel**: Formatted output with optional step display - **History Sidebar** (toggle): last 10 calculations ## 3. Functionality Specification ### Mode: Fractions - Inputs: Fraction A (numerator, denominator), Fraction B (numerator, denominator) - Operations: Add (+), Subtract (-), Multiply (×), Divide (÷), Simplify - Output: Resulting fraction in lowest terms, decimal approximation - Validation: denominators cannot be zero ### Mode: GCD-LCM - Inputs: Integer A, Integer B - Output: GCD (Euclidean algorithm), LCM - Display: Calculation steps optional ### Mode: Prime Check - Input: Single integer - Output: "Prime" / "Composite" + if composite, smallest factor ### Mode: Factorize - Input: Integer (1 to 10 trillion) - Output: Prime factorization as array, factor tree representation ### Mode: Generate Primes - Input: Range (min, max) OR Count (n primes from start) - Output: Array of primes, count, largest prime generated ### Data Handling - All math computed in TypeScript (BigInt for large numbers) - LocalStorage for history persistence only - No network calls ### Edge Cases - Zero denominators: show error, prevent calculation - Negative numbers: handle sign in fractions, reject for prime ops - Numbers exceeding safe integer: use BigInt - Empty inputs: disable calculate button ## 4. Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All five modes accessible and functional - [ ] Fraction results always reduced to lowest terms - [ ] Prime factorization handles numbers up to 10 trillion - [ ] History persists across sessions - [ ] Works completely offline - [ ] Responsive layout at minimum 600x500 - [ ] No console errors during normal operation
hint: Create a local-first desktop math utility that handles fraction arithmetic (add, subtract, multiply, divide, simplify with GCD/LCM) and prime number operations (primality test, factorization, prime generation). The module should be a self-contained calculator-style interface with mode selection, numeric inputs, operation buttons, and result display. No cloud features or external API dependencies—pure client-side computation.

title: Personal Vehicle Maintenance Log
input: Create a personal vehicle maintenance log module. It should allow adding, editing, and deleting service records. Each record includes: date, odometer reading, service type (oil change, tire rotation, brake service, fluid replacement, inspection, repair, etc.), work performed description, parts used (with quantities), cost (parts, labor, tax), and general notes. Include ability to sort by date or mileage, search notes or work performed, view total spend over time, and export to CSV. Local-first - data stored in local SQLite database, no cloud sync required.
hint: Single vehicle owner, personal use case, no fleet features, local storage only, data export capability

title: EmbeddedFontPicker
input: create an embedded font picker component in React that lets users browse system fonts, preview typography changes with editable sample text, select font weights/styles, and export the selected configuration as CSS custom properties for use in a host application
hint: focus on a portable component with clear API props for defaultFont, onChange callback, and embeddable export function; prioritize local-first font enumeration via FontFace API before falling back to a default list; include sample text that updates in real-time

title: Daily Diet Log
input: Create a simple food diary app that lets me log what I eat each day as freeform text, automatically timestamps each entry, shows a chronological list of past entries grouped by date, and can export or report entries as plain text.
hint: Local-first means no cloud dependency. Auto-timestamp is a single timestamp captured on entry creation. Report is a simple text list sorted by date, not analytics.

title: Piano Sketchpad
input: Create a single-page virtual piano chord playground as a self-contained HTML file. Requirements: 1) Visual piano keyboard spanning 2 octaves (C3-C5) with clickable keys 2) Computer keyboard mapping (QWERTY row plays white keys, number row plays black keys) 3) Audio using Web Audio API oscillator-based piano tone with envelope 4) Chord buttons for: C, Cm, C7, Cm7, Dim, Aug, sus2, sus4 - clicking a chord button highlights its notes on the keyboard and plays the chord 5) Scale selector showing major and natural minor scale degrees overlaid on keys 6) Input field where users can type note names (e.g., 'C4 E4 G4') to play custom combinations 7) Clean, minimal UI with clear key labels. No external dependencies or servers - fully local-first.
hint: Create a single HTML file with a virtual piano keyboard that plays notes via mouse click or computer keyboard input. Include chord buttons (major, minor, dim, aug, 7ths) that highlight the corresponding keys and play the chord. Add a scale selector that overlays scale degrees on the keyboard. Use Web Audio API for sound. Keep it self-contained and local-first—no server required.

title: Font Glyph Inspector
input: font name, character range (start-end hex/decimal or Unicode block name)
hint: User selects font from system list, configures range, views glyphs. Tool fetches font metadata, extracts glyphs for range, displays in paginated grid with copy actions.

title: Desktop Workflow Utility Module
input: Create a bounded npm module for desktop workflow utilities. The module should export: (1) fileOps: readFileToField, writeFieldToFile, fileToClipboard, binaryFileRead; (2) clipboardOps: copyText, pasteText, copyImage, fileToClip; (3) fieldOps: getFieldText, setFieldText, disposeFieldText; (4) textHelpers: replaceText, replaceChar, findKey, delimitedChunk, compareStrings, lastVisibleChar; (5) searchSort: sortByField, sortByItem, multiSort, findInField; (6) dialogs: modalDialog, listDialog, chooserDialog, deleteConfirm; (7) systemHelpers: checkWritePermission, getSystemFolder, getLeafName, convertDate. Use async/await. Support Node.js and Electron contexts.
hint: Focus on composing small, single-responsibility functions that work well together. Prioritize async APIs. Include TypeScript types. Do NOT build a UI framework—just the data-handling utilities.

title: Mental Math Speed Drill Module
input: Build a mental math speed drill module where users complete 25 arithmetic problems under time pressure. Include configurable difficulty (operations: add/subtract/multiply/divide), timed session tracking with millisecond precision, progress thermometer, local leaderboard storing personal best times per difficulty, and optional silent mode for faster execution.
hint: This is a single bounded drill module, not a full learning platform. Prioritize responsive input, accurate timing, and clean progress visualization. Local storage only; cloud leaderboard is optional future extension.

title: Bo's Bits: Audio Recognition Module
input: You are building a single-artist audio recognition quiz module. The module should: (1) Present short audio clips (5-15 seconds) of Bo Diddley songs, (2) Accept user guesses via text input or button selection, (3) Provide immediate feedback with answer reveal, (4) Track cumulative score across sessions, (5) Include a compact biographical panel about Bo Diddley that unlocks or highlights after correct identifications. Keep scope tight—10-15 well-chosen tracks maximum. Use local file storage for clips and scores. No network dependency required for core quiz loop.
hint: focus on authentic clip quality and biographical accuracy over quantity; build for offline-first use

title: Household Portfolio & Net Worth Tracker
input: You are building a bounded personal-finance module for a local-first household portfolio and net-worth tracker. The module should allow users to: (1) register multiple investment accounts with manual entry or CSV import, (2) maintain a holdings ledger with cost basis and purchase dates, (3) update position valuations, (4) calculate realized/unrealized capital gains using FIFO or LIFO methods, (5) compute aggregate net worth across accounts, (6) track dividend income. Prioritize local data storage and privacy. Describe the module scope, key entities, and primary flows.
hint: Include only bounded personal-finance functionality. Exclude budgeting, expense tracking, credit scores, or broad financial planning. Keep it household investment-focused and sovereign/local-first.

title: Embedded Audio Capture and Playback Module
input: Create a bounded audio utilities module that provides: (1) Audio recording functions with configurable options (sample rate, format, quality), (2) Audio playback controls (play, pause, stop, seek, volume), (3) Audio file management for saving/loading recordings, (4) Audio session configuration for platform-specific behavior including background playback support, (5) Demo examples showing integration patterns. The module should be suitable for embedding in larger applications and follow a utility/library pattern rather than a standalone app. Include clear APIs for initialization, recording lifecycle management, playback state handling, and resource cleanup.
hint: Design a lightweight, embeddable audio utility module providing recording and playback capabilities for integration into host applications

title: Sound Library Preview & Decision Module
input: sample pack name, preview clip list with URLs and metadata, full collection description, pricing/purchase link, brand voice for intro
hint: Generate a sample pack preview interface showing 5-9 representative audio clips with playback, metadata, and a purchase link to the full 90+ file collection

title: Fuzzy Record Matcher
input: Build a fuzzy record matcher as a single-purpose local-first utility. Input: a query string (one or two names/terms) + a delimited text file (CSV/TSV) containing large datasets. Processing: scan each record using typo-tolerant string matching that detects transposed letters, wrong characters, and common misspellings. Output: ranked list of matching records with similarity scores above a configurable threshold. The utility should handle the specific case of finding 'Salztbreg' when 'Salztberg' exists in the data, or 'humidifer' when 'humidifier' is the correct term.
hint: Focus strictly on fuzzy string matching over static delimited exports. Do not build a general search engine, indexing system, or web service. Use established libraries (RapidFuzz, thefuzz, Levenshtein) for the matching logic. Default threshold should filter out matches below ~60% similarity unless user overrides.

title: Interactive Biography Module - Single Subject Life & Teachings
input: Create a bounded, self-contained interactive biography module for [SUBJECT_NAME] that includes: (1) an overview/biography section with key life events, (2) a chronological timeline component showing life milestones, (3) a teachings or key works section with organized content, and (4) navigation between sections. The module should be usable as a standalone educational component, preserving the original's structured interactive approach to presenting a person's life story and core teachings.
hint: Treat as a single bounded educational component, not a full application stack. Focus on the content structure and navigation between biography, timeline, and teachings sections.

title: ReferencePad Encoder
input: reference text document (file path or paste), message text to encode/decode, shared password
hint: Apply one-time pad-style pointer modulation: generate pointers into reference text based on password-derived seed, use those pointers to select character sequences, modulate pointers with offsets also derived from reference text. Output as base64 or hex representation.

title: BulkPropertyManager
input: Create a bounded module that allows users to apply a property change to multiple selected UI elements at once. The module should: accept a selection of items; compute which properties are common to all selected items; enable/disable bulk actions based on common properties; apply a property change (like enable/disable, show/hide, set value) to all selected items in one operation.
hint: Focus on the intersection logic: only enable bulk actions when ALL selected items support that action. Include feedback showing why an action might be disabled.

title: Labanotation Reader: Interactive Notation Skill Trainer
input: I want to practice reading Labanotation. Show me the Macarena phrase from the library. Let me step through measure by measure and tap any symbol to understand what it means. I'll try to describe the movement before seeing the explanation.
hint: focus on guided skill-building through incremental symbol recognition, not content creation or notation editing

title: Tabata Interval Timer Module
input: I need a Tabata interval timer that handles timed exercise and rest intervals. It should: count down work periods and rest periods, cycle through configured rounds (standard Tabata is 20s work / 10s rest × 8 rounds), allow configurable work duration, rest duration, number of rounds, and number of cycles, provide start/pause/reset controls, play audio alert for last 3 seconds of each interval, show visual phase indicator distinguishing work vs rest vs complete states, and optionally allow saving preset configurations for quick reuse. No workout logging, no social features, no broader fitness suite.
hint: Treat as bounded timer utility. Minimal UI. Fast response. Local-first. No cloud dependency.

title: Music Timing Lab
input: I need to calculate the duration of a 32-bar section at 120 BPM, then add 3.5 seconds of silence, and determine the SMPTE timecode offset if this cue starts at 01:23:45:00 for a video sync.
hint: Calculate musical durations, tempos, bar counts, and sync offsets for composition and media production workflows

title: NY State Places Reference
input: User wants to find a place in New York State and get basic factual information about it quickly
hint: Create a bounded static reference module for state places with quick lookup and fact retrieval

title: RecursiveFileFinder Module
input: rootFolderPath: string, searchPattern: string, options?: { recursive?: boolean, caseSensitive?: boolean, maxDepth?: number }
hint: Implement a standalone module that takes a root folder path and a filename pattern (e.g., '*.txt', 'report*', '*.json'), recursively traverses all subdirectories from the root, and returns an array of absolute paths to files whose names match the pattern. Support optional flags for case sensitivity and maximum search depth. Prioritize performance and minimal dependencies.

title: Inbox Watcher Module
input: A user wants to be alerted when new items arrive in a shared mailbox, intake folder, or monitored queue without having to open the main application. They need configurable alert intervals, a system tray indicator showing unread count, and a way to mark items as seen. The module runs locally as a background service and watches an IMAP account, local folder, or API endpoint.
hint: Create a bounded module that monitors a specified inbox endpoint for new/unread items and delivers periodic alert notifications until items are acknowledged, designed for shared inboxes or intake queues where native notifications are unavailable

title: Animation Principles Lab
input: Build a single-file HTML/CSS/JS module that functions as an interactive animation learning sandbox. Include 5-6 simple animation demonstrations (e.g., fade, slide, scale, rotate, easing, bounce) where each example displays the current animation, shows editable parameters (duration, easing, direction, etc.), and provides a live preview that updates when parameters change. Each example should have a brief explanation of the animation principle it demonstrates. The module should work offline and require no server dependencies. Include a simple gallery view to browse examples and a focused detail view for experimenting with each animation.
hint: Focus on demonstrating fundamental motion design principles through editable, working examples rather than teaching comprehensive animation theory. Keep it as a focused learning sandbox/tool, not a broad course platform.

title: Pediatric Diagnostic Reasoning Trainer
input: Start a pediatric diagnostic case: Present the patient history and physical findings, then progressively order diagnostic tests and make treatment decisions while tracking costs. Submit your final diagnosis and therapy, then review the debrief with differential diagnosis and rationale.
hint: Enter a pediatric case scenario to practice cost-conscious diagnostic reasoning, or select from available cases

title: Local Command Bar with Private Vault
input: I need a quick way to launch my most-used apps AND keep a small encrypted stash of private items (passwords, API keys, notes) in one local command bar that lives on my machine.
hint: Build this as a hotkey-triggered command bar with two bounded modules: a launcher index (fuzzy search across apps/files/URLs) and a gated AES-256 encrypted vault (auth via password or system biometric). Keep it sovereign and local-first—no cloud sync. Data stays in your ~/Library/Application Support/.

title: SpeakModule
input: Convert the following text to spoken audio using the default system voice, then play it through the audio output: [USER_TEXT]
hint: Ensure TTS engine is available, check audio output device is active, handle SSML markup if present in input text

title: Temperature Scale Converter
input: You are building a bounded temperature conversion module. Create a compact HTML/CSS/JS component that accepts a numeric temperature value and a source unit (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin, or Rankine), then displays computed equivalents for all remaining units in a scannable results table. Include input validation for physically meaningful temperature ranges (e.g., no negative Kelvin values). Use local-first design—no external API calls required.
hint: Focus purely on temperature conversion math with no extraneous features. The conversion formulas are: F = C × 9/5 + 32, K = C + 273.15, R = (C + 273.15) × 9/5. Include absolute zero awareness to reject physically impossible values.

title: ScriptMan - Local Script Inventory Extractor
input: Target directory or file set (with optional glob pattern), extraction mode toggle (names only vs full signatures), statistics toggle (include occurrence counts), output format selection
hint: scan local scripts and extract function/handler names with optional occurrence counts and basic code statistics into a structured inventory report

title: Nebraska Place Reference Module
input: A local-first Nebraska place reference module — what it is, what it does, who uses it, key data points per place, and how the search and display work
hint: Focus on quick lookup workflow, realistic Nebraska place data points, and minimal dependencies

hint: Focus on the bounded workflow: contact storage → selection → label output. Avoid CRM features, calendar integration, or communication sending. Keep it local-first with optional file-based sync.

title: Maya Calendar Reference & Converter
input: Maya Calendar Converter - A local-first educational utility for converting between Gregorian and Maya calendar systems with visual glyph reference and quiz modes. Include: (1) Date input field accepting Gregorian dates and outputting Long Count, Haab, and Tzolk'in equivalents. (2) Visual glyph browser showing the 20 day signs and 19 month glyphs with clickable detail panels. (3) Quiz mode for testing date conversion and glyph identification. (4) Reference explainer pages for calendar unit relationships. All data stored locally; works fully offline. Target users: students, educators, and calendar enthusiasts seeking offline conversion and learning.
hint: Focus the module scope tightly on calendar conversion plus associated glyph reference and quiz features. Do not expand into broader Maya history, archaeology, or culture beyond what's needed to support calendar literacy. Prioritize local storage and offline operation over cloud features.

hint: Use port identifier, baud rate, databits/parity/stopbits, and optional optimization flags (low_latency, auto_irq)

title: Speech I/O Utility Module
input: Design and specify a bounded Python module called 'speech_io' that wraps text-to-speech and basic speech recognition libraries (like pyttsx3 and speech_recognition). The module should expose functions for: synthesizing text to speech with configurable voice parameters, managing voice settings (rate, volume, voice selection), and providing basic speech recognition hooks for simple voice input. Include clear invocation patterns for integrating speech output into other application workflows.
hint: Create a bounded Python module that provides unified text-to-speech synthesis and basic speech input capabilities, exposing a clean API for other application flows to leverage voice output and simple voice commands.

title: Ceramic Artifact Cataloging Module
input: artifact_identifier: string, max_dimension_mm: number, weight_g: number, exterior_surface_type: enum(glazed_unglazed_bisque, glazed_lead, glazed_tin, slipped, painted, burnished, other), exterior_color: string, interior_surface_type: enum, interior_color: string, ware_type: string, estimated_period: string, sherds_count: number, mend_relationships: array, catalog_date: date, recorder_notes: string

hint: Include configurable targets, predefined command templates, and a simple trigger interface

title: Engineering Scientific Calculator Module
input: You are a bounded scientific calculator module for engineering and technical workflows. Provide precise calculations using trigonometric functions (sine, cosine, tangent), logarithmic functions (natural log, log base 10), exponential functions, and factorials. Include a constants library with common physical constants (speed of light, Avogadro number, gas constant, gravitational acceleration, Planck constant). Support degree/radian mode toggle. Display results in decimal, scientific notation, and fraction approximation formats. Allow keyboard input and maintain a calculation history buffer. Do not expand into symbolic algebra, graphing, or programming - stay bounded as a single-purpose calculation tool.
hint: Maintain single-purpose scope: numeric scientific calculations only. Do not drift into graphing, CAS features, or programmable scripting.

title: Endpoint Directory with Smart Copy
input: A developer or support engineer needs quick access to a curated list of external service endpoints (servers, APIs, vendor contacts) with the ability to copy the endpoint in different formats depending on the target tool.

title: Delaware Places Reference Module
input: Create a minimal Delaware Places Reference module. Include: (1) An index page listing all Delaware municipalities organized alphabetically with population tier badges; (2) Individual place detail pages showing population, county, geography, and 3-5 key facts; (3) A simple header with search/filter capability. Keep the design clean, text-forward, and reference-focused. No business listings, reviews, or interactive maps required—static reference only. The scope is bounded to Delaware only.
hint: The prompt should invoke a bounded static reference tool focused on Delaware municipalities, structured as a clean index-to-detail flow with concise fact retrieval as the primary interaction pattern

title: Carmel Local Street Guide Module
input: Design a local-first mobile module that provides offline street and landmark lookup for Carmel-by-the-Sea. Users can search street names, find points of interest, and orient using local reference data - all without network dependency.
hint: Bounded city-specific offline reference tool, not a general mapping suite. Emphasize complete offline operation, local data freshness, and orientation without connectivity.

title: 2-Stroke Engine Training Module
input: Develop a standalone interactive training module that teaches the user how a 2-stroke engine works. The module must contain three bounded sections: (1) a labeled reference view showing all major engine components with interactive labels explaining each part's function; (2) a step-by-step animated or stepped visualization of the 2-stroke operating cycle (intake/compression, power/exhaust) showing how components interact during each phase; (3) a knowledge check quiz with multiple-choice questions that test understanding of component functions and the operating cycle. The module should be self-contained, run offline or in-browser without external dependencies, and provide immediate feedback on quiz answers.
hint: The module is a bounded sovereign learning unit—not a course platform or authoring tool. It serves a single learner session: study the components, understand the cycle, verify learning. No user accounts, no progress sync, no social features. The component reference should use an interactive diagram (SVG or canvas) where clicking/tapping parts reveals definitions. The cycle visualization can use animation or a manual stepping interface—either approach is acceptable. The quiz should cover both component identification and process sequence. Technical implementation could be: a single HTML file with embedded CSS/JS, a small Electron packaged app, or a contained Progressive Web App with offline capability.

title: Scholarly Annotated Text Reader
input: Create a bounded module for reading and annotating canonical literary texts with integrated scholarly notes and study aids.
hint: Focus on passage-linked annotations, margin notes, footnote handling, and text navigation. Avoid building a full learning management system - stay focused on the reading and annotation experience. Include support for scholarly features like variant comparison and glossary lookup.

title: Procedural Character Animation Scene Module
input: Write a JavaScript module that renders 1-3 animated character sprites on an HTML canvas. Each character cycles through a sprite sheet animation loop (idle, walk). Characters move randomly within canvas bounds using procedural motion - random direction changes at intervals, boundary collision, smooth interpolation. The module exports a Scene class with start(), stop(), setCharacterCount(n), and updateConfig() methods. Use requestAnimationFrame for the game loop.
hint: Include sprite sheet loading, animation frame cycling, randomized velocity vectors, boundary wrapping or bouncing, configurable animation speed per character, and clean async initialization.

title: Constitution Study Module

hint: constitutional text study with section-by-section navigation, inline annotations explaining meaning and historical context, links to relevant case law interpretations, hierarchical browse (preamble > articles > sections > amendments)
sourceTitle: The Constitution of the United States of America: The Foundation of our Country 2.1

title: Personal Page Builder

hint: You are a cartoon sound effects picker. The user wants to find and trigger short cartoon-style voice and sound clips for comedic media moments. You have access to a library of named sound clips organized by category with preview and insert actions. Wait for the user to specify what kind of sound they need (e.g., 'Witch laugh', 'Road Runner beep', 'cartoon fall') or to browse categories. When they select a clip, provide playback preview and offer to insert it into their current project or queue it for soundboard use. Offer to filter by category (voice, impact, animal, vehicle, etc.) if they want to browse. Ask if they need to hear a preview before inserting.

title: Decision Weigher
input: User provides: options to compare, criteria/factors that matter, relative importance of each criterion, pros and cons for each option under each criterion. Module calculates weighted scores and produces ranked recommendation with explanation.
hint: Local-first weighted pros/cons module with criteria weighting, option comparison scoring, and rationale export. Single-purpose personal productivity, not SaaS.

title: Trial Case Organizer
input: User provides case name, client details, opposing counsel, court dates, key deadlines, witness list, and case notes
hint: Generate a structured case workspace with deadlines, contacts, chronology, and task lists

title: Animated Quiz Module
input: Build a bounded interactive quiz module that displays a sequence of 5 questions one at a time. Each question should have 4 multiple choice options. Use Framer Motion for smooth slide transitions between questions. Track correct answers in local component state. After the final question, show a summary screen with score and feedback. The module should be completely self-contained - no external API calls or backend. Use a progress indicator to show current question number. Include a 'try again' button on the feedback screen that resets the quiz state.
hint: Use a single QuizContainer component managing state for currentQuestion, answers, and showFeedback. Questions array stored as constant in same file. Framer Motion AnimatePresence for question transitions. Simple scoring: correct = 1 point per question, display percentage on feedback.

title: Developer Scripting Console
input: Create a local-first developer console module that provides: 1) a command input bar for executing scripts/expressions, 2) an output console showing results and history, 3) a persistent scratchpad for notes and临时 code, 4) a library panel for saving and launching reusable utility scripts, 5) an integrated help/reference system, 6) a file viewer for reading text files from the filesystem, 7) export/print functionality. The module should preserve state between sessions and run entirely locally without cloud dependencies.
hint: local developer tool for running ad hoc commands, managing reusable scripts, viewing files, and keeping persistent notes in one bounded panel

title: File Repair and Partial Record Salvage Utility

hint: You are a file repair utility that salvages recoverable records from damaged workspace or application files. Given a corrupted file path, you analyze its structure, identify intact records, preview what can be recovered, and export the salvageable subset while logging damage for audit purposes.

title: DevUtils - Developer Utility Library
input: a developer wants to add common utility functions to their app without writing each one from scratch - needs dialogs, file helpers, progress indicators, UI controls, audio, system utilities

title: Endpoint Sketch
input: You are designing a bounded, local-first visual tool for individual developers to create, test, and manage simple HTTP endpoint handlers without cloud dependencies.
hint: A bounded visual tool for individual developers to define, test, and export simple HTTP endpoint handlers locally. Users draw or configure request patterns, map incoming parameters to output logic, and preview responses without deploying to a cloud service. The module includes a library of reusable handler templates (form processor, webhook receiver, static response, redirect) and a local test panel to simulate requests and validate outputs before exporting the handler code for deployment.

title: Thalassemia Diagnostic Reference Module

hint: Use a local-first desktop app for offline bilingual study drills with percentage scoring

hint: Return concise 1-2 sentence definition optimized for exam preparation and quick comprehension. Include pronunciation guidance for non-English terms. Flag ambiguous terms with common alternative meanings.

title: Technical Kanji Study Module
input: Create a local-first single-page application for studying 365 technical kanji from Basic Technical Japanese. The module should: (1) Load kanji data including character, reading, meaning, and example vocabulary; (2) Provide three sort options: by lesson/unit, by stroke count, by frequency; (3) Support hiding individual entries for self-quizzing with a reveal toggle; (4) Include a print mode that formats entries as cutable flashcards; (5) Use a clean, minimal UI without cloud dependencies.
hint: Consider using a data-driven approach where kanji are stored in a JSON file and rendered dynamically. The print view should use CSS @media print to hide UI controls and layout cards appropriately for cutting. All data stays local in the browser using localStorage for user preferences.

hint: Create a bounded module that accepts a mathematical expression string, numeric axis configuration (offset and scale), renders the plotted function on a canvas or SVG, and optionally stores frequently used formulas for quick reference. Prefer a lightweight library like Function Plot or a custom implementation over heavy charting frameworks.

title: IconForge - Lightweight Icon Asset Editor
input: local icon file or clipboard bitmap
hint: User needs to create or edit an app icon, favicon, or file icon and export it to a specific format/resource for immediate use in a project, build, or deployment workflow.

title: Binary & Memory Inspection Workbench
input: Convert address 0x00403000 to decimal and show bytes at that location; What is the trap word for _Write?; Give me an example of reading a C string from memory

patternFamily: developer-tool reference and guided utility workbench

title: Protocol Console
input: Design a bounded Protocol Console module that enables developers to connect to any remote text-based server, view live bidirectional transcript of the exact bytes sent and received (in ASCII and hex), manually compose and send line-by-line commands, and log the full session for analysis. The module should support both TCP and UDP connections, allow saving/loading connection profiles, and export session transcripts. Target users are developers and system administrators debugging network services, testing protocol implementations, or performing network diagnostics.
hint: A developer tool for testing raw TCP connections, debugging protocol implementations, and inspecting exact request/response exchanges with remote servers

title: Classroom Art Studio
input: Create a classroom drawing module for K-12 students. Features: canvas workspace with brush, color picker, eraser tools; save drawing to local portfolio; portfolio gallery view showing all saved works; optional print/export. Local-first storage (no cloud required), privacy-appropriate for schools. Bounded to drawing and portfolio viewing only - not a full creative suite.
hint: A bounded K-12 classroom drawing app with local storage and portfolio viewing. Students draw on a canvas with basic tools, save work to a personal portfolio, and teachers can browse a cumulative class gallery. Privacy-first, local storage, no social features, no cloud dependencies. Supports touch/stylus input.

title: NetworkContextDetector
input: You are a bounded, local-first utility module. Your single responsibility: detect the current network context (WiFi SSID, wired network name, VPN tunnel, or domain segment) and return it with explicit fallback states. You do NOT monitor devices, scan subnets, or build dashboards. You expose a simple API: getNetworkContext() returns { context: string, active: boolean, interface: string }. Use native OS APIs (Python's network, Node's os.networkInterfaces, or system commands) to query interface state. Return '%Network Inactive' when no active interface exists. Provide context to consuming modules so they can apply site-specific workflow defaults or routing rules. Be resilient: handle disconnected, VPN, and multi-homed scenarios gracefully.
hint: focus on the utility's core job: return current network name/identifier with clear active/inactive states. Do not expand into device discovery, monitoring dashboards, or enterprise inventory. The module should be lightweight, local-first, and easily embedded in workflow automation.

title: Genealogy Module
input: A local-first genealogy tree builder with typed relationship records, photo attachments, contextual help, and PDF/report export capabilities. Support adding root person, then creating parent/child/sibling/spouse linked records. Include image attachment per record. Provide customizeable help overlay. Support generating printable family group records with optional source citations and relationship charts. Operate as sovereign local-first module.
hint: A local-first genealogy tree builder with typed relationship records, photo attachments, contextual help, and PDF/report export capabilities.

title: Local Citation Manager
input: A personal researcher needs a local-first citation management tool that works offline, stores references securely on their device, supports CSL citation styles, and can generate formatted citations for copy-paste or direct insertion into writing software.
hint: Focus on the core loop: add reference → organize → format → output. Exclude multi-user collaboration, web sync, or library exchange features. Prioritize privacy and offline reliability.

title: Times Tables Flash

The module should: 1) Present single-digit multiplication problems (0-12 tables) one at a time, 2) Provide immediate audio/visual feedback on answers, 3) Show a simple visual progress indicator, 4) Include encouraging messages for correct answers, 5) Allow the child to select which times tables to practice, 6) Work fully offline with no account required. Design for minimal friction - no login, no ads, no social features. Focus on building fluency through low-stakes repetition with positive reinforcement.

title: Lotto Ticket Checker & Lucky Number Picker
input: The user wants to check their lottery numbers and get new number picks. They have a 6-number lottery ticket (with optional bonus). They need to (1) enter their numbers, (2) enter or fetch the winning draw, (3) see which numbers matched and any winnings, and (4) generate new lucky numbers.

When the user provides numbers from a ticket, compare them against a winning draw (which they can also input or fetch from a public API) and report which numbers matched, the prize tier if applicable, and offer to generate a new random set of numbers using atmospheric noise for true randomness. Keep a small local history of checked tickets for reference. Do not connect to external lottery databases unless the user explicitly provides the winning draw data.

title: QuoteStash - Personal Quote Library

Keep it a single-window application with modal dialogs for settings and question reveal.

title: Local-First Contact Record Keeper
input: local-first contact manager with customizable fields and sorting
hint: Design a local-first contact/record database module that stores contacts in local storage (SQLite/JSON/YAML), supports up to 4 customizable fields, enables individual field sorting, and provides simple CRUD operations without cloud dependencies

title: Meal Rotation Planner
input: Build a personal meal rotation planner as a local-first single-module tool. The user wants to: (1) maintain a catalog of meals organized by category tags like 'soups', 'salads', 'main', 'quick', etc. (2) create separate planning lists for different occasions (weeknight dinners, weekend brunch, guests, meal prep). (3) assign meals to specific days on a weekly grid. (4) track variety so they can see if they've eaten something recently. The tool should NOT include: recipe storage, grocery lists, cloud sync, or user accounts. It should work entirely offline, store data locally (JSON or SQLite), and focus purely on planning and rotation logic. Include a simple UI to add/edit meals with tags, create occasion-specific lists, drag meals onto a weekly calendar, and show a 'recently eaten' indicator for each meal.
hint: Use a lightweight framework like Tauri, Electron, or even a single HTML file with localStorage. Keep data in local JSON files. Implement category tagging, multiple lists, day assignment, and a basic variety/warning system.

title: Marble & Stone Texture Generator Module
input: Single-purpose marble texture generator that creates unique stone/marble backgrounds with adjustable vein patterns, colors, scale, and turbulence. Must include live preview, example gallery, parameter controls, help guidance, and hidden easter egg features. Export to common design formats. Local-first sovereignty, no cloud dependencies.
hint: Focus on the bounded job: generating decorative marble/rock backgrounds for designers. Avoid broad design suites. Keep it single-minded.

title: Visual UI Inventory Inspector
input: A local-first developer tool that audits a target application or design system and produces a visual inventory listing all screens/pages, their component element counts (buttons, fields, controls), embedded resources, and visual thumbnails
hint: Focus on bounded enumeration and visual documentation rather than editing or modification. Single-target audit workflow.

title: AdLens: Critical Media Literacy Module
input: I need an interactive classroom module that teaches students to critically analyze TV and digital advertising. It should include a structured analysis framework students apply to real ads, with guided breakdown exercises, some interactive quiz/check elements, and instructor materials for teaching the lesson.
hint: Focus on the bounded teaching workflow: introduce the analysis framework, have students apply it to real ad examples with guided exercises, include a comprehension check, and provide lesson guidance for instructors.

title: PairRank: Pairwise Shortlist Prioritizer
input: I need to rank a list of options by comparing them in pairs and selecting the best from each pair to build a ranked shortlist
hint: the user provides a list of options (typically 3-15), the module guides them through head-to-head comparisons, tracks wins, and produces a ranked result

title: Local Inventory Ledger
input: A small business owner needs to track inventory items with their costs and selling prices, maintain current stock counts, record purchases and sales, calculate Cost of Goods Sold using either FIFO or weighted average methods, and generate period reports showing stock valuation, COGS, gross margins, and turnover. The module should store data locally (SQLite database or local files), require no internet connection, support import/export of item catalogs via CSV, and generate printable period reports. It is a bounded local-first module, not a SaaS platform or suite.
hint: Focus on the core recordkeeping workflow: item records, stock movements, COGS calculation, and period reporting. Keep it bounded—no multi-user sync, no e-commerce integration, no vendor portal. The value is in accurate COGS tracking and simple performance reports for a single-location small business.

title: ParameterTuner
input: A user needs to adjust multiple numeric parameters (like volume, opacity, zoom, speed) while working in another application or view without switching contexts
hint: The module should float above other content, contain 1-8 sliders with clear labels and current values, update in real-time, and optionally bind to external state

title: FontCard.local
input: user needs to browse and preview locally-installed fonts in discrete card records, customize preview text, view font metadata, and select fonts for use in external design tools
hint: local-first font browser with card-based preview, custom sample text per card, metadata inspection, and design-tool export

title: Litigation Document Review Workspace
input: Case folder or document collection for litigation matter
hint: Import transcripts, depositions, interrogatories, discovery documents; search across all loaded text; extract and save cited passages with citations intact; create linked notes keyed to date, document, or subject

title: KeyState Utility Module
input: Create a keyboard input utility module that translates virtual keycodes to character keys, provides functions to check if specific keys are currently down, detects modifier key states (control, shift, alt, command), and allows flushing the key event buffer to prevent stale input from accumulating.
hint: Focus on state polling rather than event-driven callbacks where API limitations require it. Include both numeric keycode translation and string-based key identification. The module should be useful for hotkey registration, keyboard-driven UI navigation, and input validation.

title: Option Intrinsic Value Calculator
input: user provides: optionType (call/put), strikePrice (numeric), underlyingCurrentPrice (numeric)
hint: focus only on intrinsic value (not time value/extrinsic), return zero for OTM options

title: Household Contact Manager
input: I want to keep a personal directory of my contacts—friends, family, colleagues—with their names, addresses, phone numbers, emails, and websites. I need to track birthdays and anniversaries, organize them into categories like "Family," "Friends," "Work," add notes about each person, attach photos, and print mailing labels and contact sheets. Everything should stay local on my device.
hint: The bounded domain is personal/household contact management, not business CRM or sales pipeline. Exclude: lead scoring, deal stages, pipeline analytics, team collaboration, cloud sync. Include: local storage, important date reminders, label printing, category filtering, photo display.

title: DBShrink - SQLite File Compaction Utility
input: Build a minimal local-first SQLite database compaction tool that selects a .db file, calculates potential space savings, runs the VACUUM command, and reports results. Focus on CLI experience with a single-purpose bounded scope.
hint: Use Python's sqlite3 module or CLI wrapper; argparse for CLI; include --dry-run flag; handle locked database errors gracefully

title: Content-Aware Personal Movie Catalog
input: content advisory movie database with filters and custom entries
hint: local-first personal movie catalog with faceted filtering on content attributes (violence, nudity, language, sexual content), searchable metadata fields, custom user entries, bulk data pack import/export, and printable filtered result lists for family viewing decisions

title: QuickEdit Module
input: A user needs to quickly open a plain text file, make targeted edits, and save changes back—all without launching a heavy editor or leaving their current context.
hint: Think: lightweight embeddable editor, local file system access, no cloud required, instant load time, focused only on the open-edit-save workflow.

title: Document Media Composer Module
input: Place, scale, import, and capture graphics for use in visual layouts and printed reports
hint: Focus on bounded document composition: image placement within templates, screen-to-clipboard capture, media library integration for reusable assets. Avoid generic image editors—target report/template workflows.

title: LocalThumbnailGenerator
input: image source (file path, buffer, or stream)
hint: A focused module that accepts an image input and produces a resized derivative optimized for rapid preview rendering in list views and asset browsers, with optional metadata preservation and format standardization.

title: AFI Media Technology Workshop Catalog
input: Enumerate available AFI non-degree media technology workshops for the current season with discipline categories, target audience levels, workshop duration/format, prerequisite requirements, and registration/enrollment pathways for prospective students.
hint: Focus on continuing education and professional development courses, not degree programs. Include both in-person and any online/hybrid formats if available. Distinguish between courses open to general public vs. entertainment industry professionals.

title: Citation Import Pipeline Module
input: raw citation export from MedLine, PubMed, or bibliographic database
hint: Process and clean raw citation metadata for reference manager import, with author field validation and automated error detection

title: Profile-Based Travel Time Calculator
input: profile_name, distance_value, distance_unit, speed_factor
hint: output_format: {travel_time: "3.5 days", profile: "NCC-1701-D", distance: 23, unit: "light-years", speed: "Warp 9"}

title: Input History Buffer
input: Implement a local-first input history component with the following constraints: (1) entries are added on form/command submission, (2) a configurable maximum size (default 50), (3) oldest entry evicted when limit reached (FIFO), (4) dropdown displays recent entries sorted newest-first, (5) arrow key navigation to cycle through history, (6) entries stored in browser localStorage keyed by input field name. Return only the bounded module code without SaaS, authentication, or cloud sync.
hint: Focus on the history management logic and the UI dropdown. Avoid drift toward browser autocomplete or generic storage solutions. The module should be embeddable and work offline.

title: Russian Vocabulary Drill Module (Local-First)
input: Given a vocabulary deck of Russian terms (Cyrillic → English gloss), serve items one at a time in drill sequence, reveal on demand, capture correctness, advance to next item, maintain progress state per item, export progress summary on request
hint: prefer local storage or in-memory state; optional spaced-repetition weighting; single bounded query endpoint; avoid full-sync or multi-deck complexity; preserve scope to Russian vocabulary drill only

title: Wind Chill Safety Calculator
input: Build a bounded local-first wind chill safety calculator module
hint: The prompt should specify: (1) dual-unit input for temperature and wind speed, (2) NWS 2001 wind chill formula implementation, (3) frostbite risk display with color coding, (4) validity bounds warning when conditions are outside calculator range, (5) offline-first with no external API dependencies. Avoid: adding weather forecasts, location services, or broader weather functionality. Keep it a single-purpose cold-risk assessment tool.

title: Thoroughbred Race Handicapping Calculator
input: raceCard
hint: Provide race entrants with their historical performance data including: horse names, recent finish positions, final times, Breeze figures, class level of past races, E1/E2 pace ratings, and trainer statistics. The module will output a ranked list of entrants with scores and visualization.

title: French Early Literacy Lesson Module
input: French alphabet letter presentation with pronunciation audio; sight word image-to-word matching; progressive reading exercises with feedback
hint: Use bounded local-first architecture. Core lesson flow: alphabet intro -> word matching -> guided reading. Limited content set (like original demo). Child-safe. No cloud. Immersion-school ready.

title: Personal Investment Portfolio Ledger
input: User wants to add a new stock holding, record quarterly dividend received, view YTD performance, and export tax report
hint: A bounded personal finance module for recording investment holdings and income events, with local-only data storage and CSV import/export. Not a cloud-connected broker sync—focus on user-controlled ledger with export capabilities.

title: SystemUtils - PowerShell Utility Cmdlet Module
input: A PowerShell user needs to perform file operations, path parsing, system queries, or text formatting tasks in their automation scripts but finds the built-in cmdlets cumbersome or incomplete for these specific utility actions.
hint: Focus on single-responsibility cmdlets that do one thing well - Get-FileEOF, Get-PathComponent, Get-SystemFolder, Convert-Delimiter, Set-SystemVolume, etc. These should be composable building blocks.

title: Script Playground for Educators
input: create an interactive script example gallery where educators can browse working code samples organized by classroom task (quiz generation, flashcard decks, student response collection, simple simulations), click any example to see it run in a live preview pane, view the editable source code alongside, read brief explanations of key commands used, copy reusable snippets, and experiment by modifying code in a sandbox to see changes instantly reflected in the preview
hint: the workflow mirrors HyperCard's inspect-and-adapt model: see it work → see how it works → make it your own

title: Visual HTML Editor Module
input: A bounded, embeddable WYSIWYG HTML editor module in TypeScript/React that runs client-side with no external dependencies.
