Include visual editing mode, raw HTML source toggle, live preview in sandboxed iframe, and export to file or clipboard. Toolbar should be configurable. No server required - fully local-first.
hint: Use ProseMirror or Tiptap as editor engine foundation. Isolate preview in sandboxed iframe. Keep module under 50KB gzipped. Should work offline.
sourceTitle: WYSISWYG: What You See is Sorta What You Get

title: Verified File Copy Module
input: I need to copy a folder of large GIS data files (approximately 16TB total) from one location to another with guaranteed integrity. The current workflow using XCopy /v or WinDiff is too slow. I need: 1) Select source directory, 2) Select destination, 3) Choose files to copy, 4) Copy with real-time or post-copy hash verification using SHA-256, 5) Generate integrity report showing which files passed/failed verification, 6) Option to retry failed copies automatically.
hint: A local-first module for copying files with built-in hash verification. Select source and destination, choose files, run copy with automatic checksum generation and comparison. Ideal for backups, archival, and data delivery where integrity is critical.

title: Mailbox Extractor: Email Archive to Tabular Converter
input: email archive files or directory path
hint: parse all .mbx or mbox files in directory, extract: From, To, Subject, Date, Body preview, output to indexed CSV with ISO date normalization

title: DevTool Patch Manager - Local Update Workflow Module
input: The user wants a bounded local-first module for managing developer tool updates. Input would include: current tool version, target version, patch manifest with bug fixes, compatibility test results, and upgrade escalation rules for versions outside the supported update path.
hint: Design a CLI module for applying targeted patches and version updates to local development tools, with eligibility checking, compatibility verification after patch application, and escalation paths for unsupported version migrations

title: Problem-Solving Reflection Module
input: [userProfile] | [taskType] | [reflectionMode]
hint: Design a self-contained assessment module where a learner completes a defined problem-solving task (e.g., riddle, pattern puzzle, spatial reasoning) and then reflects on their solution process. The system captures behavioral data (time to solution, attempt sequence, hint usage) and contrasts it against the learner's self-reported learning approach (trial-and-error vs sudden insight). Output includes a personalized summary that gently surfaces the tension between the two psychological theories without prescribing a conclusion. Avoid grading or scoring; focus on metacognitive awareness.

title: Columbia River Reservoir Operations Simulator
input: Create a local‑first Python application that lets water‑resource planners define Columbia‑River reservoir parameters (capacity, initial storage, environmental flow minima, flood‑control thresholds, hydropower target, recreation pool target), set release‑policy rules (e.g., constant release, proportional to inflow, target‑storage, demand‑driven), run a yearly time‑step mass‑balance simulation using historical or synthetic inflow series, compute performance metrics for each objective (salmon habitat index, flood‑risk days, energy generation, recreation pool‑level days), store scenario definitions in a local SQLite database, produce an interactive Streamlit dashboard with Plotly time‑series plots for storage, releases, and objective scores, allow side‑by‑side scenario comparison, and export a printable PDF report showing yearly operation trends and relative benefit charts. The tool must run offline, require no cloud services, and be installable via pip and a single‑entry script.
hint: The prompt should be bounded, explicit about local‑first constraints, specify required sub‑modules, mention the data flow, and request both interactive UI and static report generation. It should not ask for cloud storage, multi‑user collaboration, or external API integrations.

title: Desktop Automation Toolkit
input: [actionType] - one of [file_ops,dialog,text_transform,list_ops,clipboard_media,system_meta]
hint: Include input parameters needed for the specific action (e.g., source file, destination, search pattern, prompt text)

title: Squash Referee Scoreboard
input: Build a bounded squash referee scoreboard module: single-screen app showing current match score visible through court glass, serve-side indicator, point/let/conduct buttons, match-end detection for various scoring formats (PAR, handout, best-of-3/5, first-to-N), undo capability, and text-to-speech announcements of score in French and English. Must support singles and doubles serving rules.
hint: MUST stay bounded: only referee scoreboard functions, no player registration database, no league management, no social features. Local-first device storage.

title: Encounter Resolution Assistant
input: user provides: combatant name, initiative roll, AC, HP, position coordinates (optional), creature type/token reference
hint: user needs: add/remove combatants, roll/enter initiative, advance turn, track current round, place/move tokens on grid

title: Virtualized Viewport with Boundary-Aware Rendering
input: Implement a virtualized list component that renders only visible items plus a configurable overscan buffer. Must handle boundary conditions gracefully—scroll position clamped to dataset limits, empty state handling at start/end, no flicker during rapid scrolling. Use requestAnimationFrame or equivalent for render coalescing.
hint: Prefer explicit boundary checks using Math.min/Math.max over implicit clamping. Include overscan configuration (default 3-5 items). Support fixed-height items for simplicity; mention variable-height as extension.

title: Local File Malware Remediation Utility
input: [project-directory-path]
hint: User selects a project or document directory to scan. The tool detects self-replicating code (macro viruses, script-based worms, infected files) and presents a list of detected threats. User can quarantine, remove, or restore files. Post-clean verification confirms remediation success.

title: TermBank Quiz Generator
input: Create a bounded module for educators to build custom terminology quizzes. Teachers should be able to: (1) Create and manage a personal glossary of term-definition pairs, (2) Import existing term sets, (3) Generate quizzes by selecting terms from their glossary, (4) Choose question formats (multiple choice, definition matching, fill-in-blank), (5) Export quizzes to shareable formats. The module is local-first and sovereign - no cloud dependency required.
hint: Design a local-first vocabulary quiz builder where teachers maintain private term glossaries and generate randomized quizzes. Include term CRUD, quiz generation with configurable question types, and export to standard formats. Prioritize teacher control over terms and privacy of student data.

title: Recursive Geometry Explorer
input: Generate and tweak recursive geometric patterns from presets to explore recursion visually
hint: Focus on outline-based fractals (Koch curves, snowflakes, dragon curves, trees) rendered as vector paths with live parameter adjustment. Single-page, no account required. Educational but not a full curriculum.

title: Local Catalog Browser
input: local flat-file catalog import and searchable metadata browser, single-user, offline-first, sovereign module
hint: The module should accept local flat files, parse them into a local index, and provide search and detail views. It should work fully offline and be bounded to catalog import and browsing only.

title: Video Timed Text Editor
input: video file or existing timed text file
hint: author timestamped text entries with start/end times, assign track type, preview against video, export to standard formats

title: TTL-CMOS Logic Reference & Simulator
input: I need to quickly reference standard TTL and CMOS logic circuits, view their truth tables and electrical characteristics, and run basic simulations to verify behavior before building hardware.
hint: A bounded reference and simulation tool for looking up TTL and CMOS logic circuits, viewing detailed specifications, running parameterized behavior simulations, and comparing family characteristics. Targets hardware engineers, students, and technicians who need quick circuit lookup and behavior verification before building or troubleshooting.

title: Spanish Math Drills - Bilingual Early Childhood Adaptive Quiz Module
input: [childAge, currentSkillLevel]
hint: The module generates a single math problem (visual + Spanish audio), presents 3 multiple-choice answers, tracks attempts, delivers animated remediation on second failure, requires self-correction before proceeding, and adapts difficulty based on success rate.

title: TermDrill: Technical Vocabulary Practice Module
input: Create a local-first vocabulary quiz module where users practice computer and technical terms with immediate feedback. Include a bundled term bank of 100+ technical terms. Each quiz session randomizes question order. After answering, show correct/incorrect status with explanation if wrong. Track session score. Allow users to import custom term lists. Keep it offline-capable and self-contained.
hint: focus on local storage, randomized question flow, instant feedback display, and session history - avoid server dependencies

title: ScriptHeader
input: script file path or file content
hint: Extract and present a concise symbol map of the input script showing all defined handlers, functions, and global declarations organized by category.

title: ClassroomMail: Local-First School Messaging Module
input: Design a local-first classroom messaging module where students and teachers can send, receive, and manage messages within a school-hosted environment. Include: (1) teacher admin panel for creating student accounts and monitoring activity, (2) student inbox with read/unread and threading, (3) compose/send flow with recipient selection, (4) local storage (no external network calls). Keep it bounded, privacy-focused, and suitable for teaching email literacy.
hint: Design a bounded, local-first classroom messaging module for students and teachers to practice email workflows in a controlled, offline-capable environment. Include inbox, compose, and teacher admin submodules. Emphasize privacy (no external calls), role-based access, and educational scaffolding.

title: Ivory Towers: Addition Adventure
input: Design a self-contained mobile module for early-elementary addition fact practice where children solve addition problems (sums 0-20, focus on 1st-2nd grade curriculum) presented by animated character hazards along a linear journey path. Correct answers earn points and advance progress; reaching milestone thresholds unlocks one of 15 decorative animation/sound rewards. No time pressure, no failure states, no social features. Fully offline-capable after download with local-first data storage. The reward catalog is viewable at any time and contains unlockable animations children can tap to replay for fun.
hint: Output the bounded module spec: 1) Problem generation rules for addition facts with plausible wrong-answer selection, 2) Progression system showing path advancement and milestone triggers, 3) Reward unlock logic (fixed 15-item catalog, unlock criteria), 4) Local persistence schema for points and unlock states, 5) Feedback UX for correct/incorrect answers, 6) No external API dependencies or account requirements.

title: Micro-Interaction Icon Pack Module
input: User needs to add visual interface assets (icons, micro-animations) to improve project navigation and presentation. User browses/searches, previews animated and static variants, selects assets, exports in chosen format (SVG/PNG/React/HTML/Vue/Svelte), and inserts into project or sends to Figma.
hint: You are a component. Your core actions are: (1) browse and search icon assets by category/style/animation type (2) preview static and animated variants with playback controls (3) select icons and export in multiple formats (SVG/PNG/React/HTML/Vue/Svelte) (4) insert directly into bound project or send to external design tool via plugin

title: LineCraft Text Transformer
input: Transform raw text content by applying batch operations: find and replace patterns, add or remove line numbers, strip or attach prefixes/suffixes to lines, remove unwanted characters or whitespace, and export a summary report of all transformations applied.
hint: A focused text transformation utility for developers, writers, and data workers who need repeatable line-level text cleanup without full IDE or spreadsheet overhead

title: KeyModifier
input: query modifier keys
hint: input: optional platform target, output: bitmask of currently pressed modifier keys (Ctrl, Shift, Alt, Meta/Super)

title: Recipe Keeper Desktop
input: Design a bounded desktop module for a local-first recipe collection manager that allows users to add, categorize, browse, search, import/export in JSON or CSV format, and print individual recipes or generate PDF cookbooks. The module should work entirely offline with a simple help system.
hint: local-first recipe manager with import/export and print capabilities

title: Digital Navigator: Internet Essentials Module
input: Design a bounded self-paced module that helps internet newcomers complete foundational tasks: setting up an email account, using a web browser safely, navigating to common sites, understanding basic terminology, and applying online safety practices. Structure as interactive reference cards with clear steps, screenshots, and a quick-lookup glossary.
hint: Focus on one bounded module—digital literacy onboarding—not a broad curriculum suite. Prioritize task completion over theory. Include a cheat sheet/quick reference card as the final deliverable.

title: Background Audio Controller Module
input: Embed a non-blocking audio player that plays continuous background music while allowing users to interact with app buttons, fields, and other UI elements without audio interruption
hint: Use platform audio APIs with background mode enabled, decouple audio thread from UI thread, provide async playback methods, handle audio session interruptions gracefully

title: Apple Event Inspector
input: The user's specific automation debugging task: target application name/signature, event class/code, direct parameter data or script source, expected outcome or error being debugged
hint: The prompt should capture the raw IPC message or script being tested, the target application context, and what the user is trying to debug or verify in the response

title: MIDI Sample Trigger
input: Create a single-window standalone desktop application that loads audio samples (WAV, AIFF, MP3) and plays them when receiving MIDI notes. Users drag-drop samples onto pad slots, click a pad to arm MIDI learn and press a key to map it. Each pad has a small vertical fader for volume and a horizontal slider for pitch shift. No sequencer - pure sample triggering only. Include basic transport (stop all, panic). Works offline, no account required.
hint: Think simple drum pad machine but for arbitrary samples, triggered by external MIDI controller, not a full DAW or plugin

title: Vocabulary Sentence Drill
input: Generate a vocabulary sentence drill tool that takes a user-provided word list and constructs sentences using configurable sentence templates, then reads them aloud using system text-to-speech. It should support continuous playback mode for drilling, adjustable speech rate, and vocabulary management (add, edit, delete word lists). The tool should be local-first with no cloud dependency.
hint: One bounded desktop app focusing narrowly on vocabulary drilling with TTS - not a full LMS or course platform. Avoid generic SaaS features. Prioritize offline functionality and simple, focused workflow.

title: Vocabulary Drill & Quiz Module
input: Build a vocabulary drill and quiz module allowing users to (1) create flashcard decks with term/definition pairs, (2) import existing deck files in JSON/CSV format, (3) run quiz sessions presenting cards and collecting user self-assessment of correct/incorrect, (4) display final percentage score and list missed cards for review. All data stored locally.
hint: The module is bounded - NOT a full LMS, NOT spaced repetition, NOT cloud-based. Just deck authoring + quiz execution + percentage scoring, running entirely on local device storage.

title: French-English Study Dictionary with Grammar Reference
input: Design a bounded French-English study dictionary module that enables learners to quickly look up vocabulary and immediately see: English meaning, noun gender (m/f), adjective feminine form transformations, and irregular verb conjugation references. The module should support fast search, save favorite terms for review, and include quick grammar reference cards for noun gender rules and common adjective transformations. Prioritize speed and clarity for classroom and homework use. Do not expand into a full language learning suite—stay focused on instant lookup and reference.
hint: Focus on the core lookup workflow: search term → get translation + gender/form info. Keep grammar references fast and scannable. No full textbook content.

title: Lemonade Stand Simulator
input: Create a single bounded HTML/JS simulation module for a classroom lemonade stand business simulation. The module should allow students to make purchasing decisions (cups, ingredients), production decisions (how much to make), and pricing decisions (price per cup). It must include a sales engine that calculates demand based on price and random weather, then displays profit/loss feedback. Include 10 rounds of gameplay with a scoreboard. No backend required - pure client-side.
hint: Use vanilla JavaScript with a clean card-based UI. Include weather randomization affecting demand. Track daily profit and cumulative score. Simple but engaging feedback loop.

title: Steroid Structure Reference Module (SteroidRef)
input: I need to build a specialized steroid compound reference module for biochemistry teaching and study. It should allow users to browse, search, and compare steroid structures with classifications (anabolics, catabolics, cardenolides, bufadienolides, hormones, steroidal vitamins, anti-inflammatories). The module needs structure visualization, compound details, and educational context. Design a bounded module with a local-first data architecture.
hint: focus on compound record structure, classification taxonomy, structure rendering approach (SMILES/MOL format), search indexing strategy, comparison view logic, and educational annotation fields. Keep bounded to steroid compounds only - do not expand to general biochemistry database.

title: Data Compression Utility Module
input: Define the public API surface for a compression utility module. Include functions: compressFile(buffer, algorithm), decompressFile(buffer, algorithm), compressString(text, algorithm), decompressString(compressed, algorithm). Support at least gzip and brotli for binary data, and lz-string style for strings. Consider streaming for large files. Document file size thresholds where compression is effective.
hint: Create a bounded utility module that exposes compress/decompress actions for files, binary assets (images, fonts), and strings. Include sync and async variants. Support common algorithms like gzip, brotli, and LZ-based string compression. Target use cases: reducing bundle size for distribution, compressing user-generated content before storage, reducing network payload sizes, and handling text payloads in local-first apps.

title: Sample Sketchpad
input: You are designing a lightweight browser-based music composition tool that lets users quickly create simple melodies and beats using point-and-click note entry, assign custom samples or instruments to tracks, save compositions locally, and export/import song data. The tool should be a focused sketchpad, not a full DAW. Include: 1) A piano-roll or step-grid note entry surface for composing melodies and rhythms. 2) Transport controls (play, pause, stop, loop) for previewing compositions. 3) An instrument/sample picker that allows users to assign different sounds to different tracks or steps. 4) A local composition library for saving and retrieving saved works. 5) Import/export functionality for exchanging composition files with other users or tools.
hint: focus on a bounded, local-first tool that emphasizes quick ideation and sketch-style composition over full production capabilities. Avoid features like notation rendering, real-time collaboration, or cloud sync - these belong in separate modules.

title: Idea Forge
input: User provides: (1) the topic, goal, or problem, (2) the category/mode (slogan, headline, product name, fictional plot, general problem solving), (3) any constraints or preferences. You generate a diverse list of candidate ideas, then assist with refinement, prioritization, and plain-text export.
hint: You are an idea generation assistant. Given a topic and category (slogan, headline, name, plot, problem-solving), generate a list of candidate ideas. Allow user to select favorites, refine them, add notes, and export selected ideas as plain text for use in external writing tools.

title: Serial Device Terminal Kit
input: Write a bounded module that provides serial device communication capabilities: connect to a serial port (USB-Serial, RS-232), open a terminal-style interface for interactive command exchange, send and receive data frames, include example protocol command handlers (status query, configuration read/write, data polling), manage connection state, and provide example integrations for common serial devices. The module should be usable as a local developer tool or embedded library, not a full terminal emulator suite.
hint: Focus on the developer use case: programmatically or interactively communicating with a connected serial device for testing, monitoring, or device control. Include terminal-style read/write but avoid building a complete terminal emulator UI.

title: LucidCycle
input: user provides dream content, selects/creates dream signs, configures alarm times, triggers manual reality-check cue
hint: local-first SQLite-based storage, no cloud sync required for core function, alarm scheduling via native OS notification APIs, optional export for backup

title: Personal Nutrient Target Calculator
input: user profile (age, sex, pregnancy/lactation status, activity level)
hint: focus on profile-based recommendation engine, not tracking or logging

title: Literal German Explorer
input: category selection
hint: Create a module that lets users browse categories of German words, view each term with its literal English translation and a brief explanatory note, with the goal of sparking curiosity about language. Use a category index as entry point and example cards with source term, literal rendering, and contextual explanation. Prioritize visual simplicity and browsability over gamification or tracking.

title: SceneBoard
input: Users need a scene board to order screenplay scenes as cards and a character reference to look up character details while drafting
hint: Draft a bounded module prompt that focuses on scene card management and character reference as two integrated but distinct submodules within a single writing workflow tool. The prompt should describe a two-pane interface where users can create scene cards with standard screenplay headings (INT/EXT, location, time), reorder them via drag-drop, and maintain character profiles accessible via a sidebar or quick-lookup. Emphasize local-first data ownership and simple markdown/JSON export rather than cloud sync. Include that scene cards should reference which characters appear in them, enabling bidirectional links. Avoid features like collaboration, revision tracking, or formatting automation—focus purely on structural organization and reference.

title: Camera Sim: Exposure & DOF Trainer
input: You are designing a standalone beginner photography training tool that runs entirely in a web browser. Create a single HTML/CSS/JS module containing: 1) A visual preview area that renders a simulated photo showing depth-of-field blur and brightness based on camera settings; 2) A control panel with sliders for f/stop (aperture), focal length, ISO (film speed), shutter speed, and subject distance; 3) An exposure meter indicator showing whether the current settings produce underexposed, correct, or overexposed results; 4) A simple guided walkthrough or tooltips that explain what each setting does; 5) Scene presets (outdoor sunny, indoor, low light, portrait) that pre-configure lighting conditions. The tool should run locally without any server dependency. Write complete functional code.
hint: A beginner photography learning tool where users adjust f/stop, focal length, ISO, and distance sliders to see real-time changes in depth of field blur and exposure on a simulated photo; includes a meter showing correct/under/over exposure and a short guided tutorial.

title: Personal Extension Library Manager
input: I need to find a [capability] extension for [tool/environment], and I want to track which ones I've tried with personal notes about what worked or didn't
hint: The module accepts a capability query and returns matching extensions from the local registry, allowing the user to annotate and track their evaluation progress

title: Visual Navigator Module
input: A multi-page document, screen flow, wireframe set, or sequential content that needs visual navigation overview
hint: Build a standalone thumbnail navigator that renders previews of all pages/screens in a grid or strip, highlights current position, and enables direct click-to-jump navigation. Prioritize local-first rendering and minimal external dependencies.

title: Movement Notation Trainer
input: You are an interactive movement notation learning module. Present a Labanotation or similar movement notation symbol, then display its corresponding animated figure in step-by-step playback. Allow the user to toggle between front/side/top viewpoints. Include a frame-advance control so users can study each beat of the notation. Provide a symbol dictionary accessible on demand. After demonstrating a phrase, hide the notation and ask the user to identify the movement from the animation, then reveal the notation for self-verification. Include a "walking in a square" example and a "circling phrase" example as practice exercises.
hint: bounded to notation interpretation training only - no choreography creation, no video recording, no social features, no broad dance content library

title: DecisionFlow Studio
input: Create a decision tree for [domain] that guides users through structured questions to reach specific conclusions based on their answers, with the ability to export standalone guided workflows
hint: Design a decision-tree builder where experts author question-and-answer flows that guide end users to conclusions, supporting rich media at endpoints

title: Company Profile Manager for Invoicing
input: user provides company details once
hint: generates populated invoice/statement preview with company info embedded

title: Static Link Directory Generator
input: Generate a static link directory from a curated collection of resources organized by category taxonomy using page templates.
hint: Focus on: (1) Link CRUD with metadata, (2) Hierarchical category management, (3) Template-based page generation, (4) Static HTML output suitable for hosting on any web server.

title: Flash Your IQ: Timed Self-Assessment Module
input: Create a self-contained HTML/CSS/JS module that runs a timed 10-question multiple-choice quiz entirely in the browser. Each question displays for 10 seconds with a visible countdown. After the timer expires or the user selects an answer, the module immediately advances to the next question. At the end of the quiz, the module calculates a score (0-10), maps it to a 5-tier ranking scale, and displays the user's final rank with a horizontal bar visualization showing their position on the scale. Use vanilla JavaScript and minimal CSS—no external libraries.
hint: Design a lightweight, client-side timed MCQ module. Users see one question at a time with a countdown (6-12s). After selecting one of three answers—or running out of time—the module records the response, advances to the next question, and at the end displays a cumulative score on a visual 'IQ Scale' ranging from a low comedic label to a high ranking. The entire module should be embeddable as a single HTML/JS artifact with no external API dependencies.

title: Phonics Reader - Single Story Module
input: Create a bounded, single-story reading practice module for beginning readers (ages 4-7). The module should: - Display one illustrated story page with large, decodable text (CVC words, simple blends) - Highlight each word in synchronization with recorded narration - Allow children to tap any word to hear that word's sound/phonic pronunciation - Use simple flip-card style page transitions - Include a "repeat" button for each page - Track reading progress locally (pages completed) - Show a completion celebration when the story is finished - Work fully offline after initial content load - Use child-friendly UI: large touch targets, bright colors, minimal text - Include one complete story (~8-12 pages) with consistent illustration style - No ads, no accounts, no external links
hint: Focus on the word-by-word reading guidance mechanic, not gamification or progression systems. This is a learning tool, not a game. Keep it bounded to ONE story.

title: LinkForge: Local-First Bidirectional Knowledge Base
input: A directory path containing markdown files with [[wikilinks]] or standard markdown links, plus optional HTML-like text files for import
hint: Build a Python module that indexes markdown files, extracts bidirectional links into a graph structure (adjacency list or property graph), provides API endpoints for link queries (backlinks, forward links, broken links), includes an import transformer for HTML-like source files to markdown with link remapping, and offers a simple cross-reference visualization endpoint

title: HierarchicalSelect Module
input: Build a bounded HierarchicalSelect UI module that lets users browse and pick from arbitrarily deep menu structures with dynamic data, icons, and configurable styling.
hint: Focus on the recursive rendering logic, state management for nested selections, and dynamic data loading hooks. Avoid building a full navigation system—stay bounded to selection from tree data.

title: Session Timeout Manager Module
input: Given a shared workstation, terminal server session, kiosk app, or conference room computer that multiple users access, I need a module that: (1) Tracks session idle time and total session duration against configurable limits, (2) Displays a warning dialog when approaching timeout threshold, (3) Allows user to extend session with a single action, (4) Automatically releases session resources and logs user out when timeout is reached or user ignores warning, (5) Provides admin configuration for timeout durations, warning intervals, and behavior policies
hint: Target: shared workstation, RDS, kiosk, or multi-user terminal access control. Output: session state machine with idle detection, warning countdown UI, extend-action handler, auto-logout controller, and configurable admin settings.
patternFamily: session management and shared-resource access control

title: Europe Geography Study Module
input: Create a local-first Europe geography study module with learn and quiz modes for countries, capitals, flags, and major regions. Include reference browser with searchable index and adaptive quiz using spaced repetition. Export/import deck capability.
hint: Focus on bounded single-topic geography. Prioritize offline functionality and spaced repetition algorithm over gamification. Keep data set fixed to European countries and territories.

title: Frame-Accurate SFX Cue Sequencer
input: the user loads a video file, imports short audio clips from a folder, places each clip onto the frame grid at a specific position, plays back to verify sync, exports cue list or burns into video
hint: frame-accurate cue placement for short SFX against video - not a full DAW or composition tool, just simple trigger placement

title: DelimiterBalance CLI
input: Create a single-purpose command-line tool that takes a string or file path as input and checks whether all parentheses (), brackets [], and braces {} are properly balanced. The tool should return exit code 0 for balanced input and exit code 1 for unbalanced input, with a clear message indicating the first mismatch position if found. Include support for: 1) reading from stdin, 2) reading from a file argument, 3) showing the line and column number of the first unbalanced delimiter. Keep it lightweight with zero external dependencies beyond the standard library.
hint: Build a minimal CLI tool that validates balanced parentheses, brackets, and braces in any text or code snippet before build/compile

title: Flash IQ/25: Timed Trivia Quiz Module
input: a timed trivia quiz module that presents multiple-choice questions with countdown timer, calculates score from correct answers, maps score to an IQ-style rating scale (e.g., 'Saddam's Clone' to 'Genius'), provides instant feedback after each answer, shows end-state results with rating, and allows quiz restart - all runs locally in-browser with no external dependencies or server calls
hint: Implement as a self-contained HTML/CSS/JS module using localStorage for high score persistence if needed. Use setInterval for timer, array for question bank, simple score-to-rating mapping function. Questions should cover diverse categories (geography, history, politics, literature, science, sports, arts). Timer configurable per-question (default 6-12 seconds). UI should show question, 3 answer buttons, countdown timer, current score, and final rating screen.

title: ShelveIt: Library Call Number Sorting Trainer
input: self-paced call number sorting drill with immediate feedback, classification system toggle (Dewey/LCC), streak-based gamification, completion celebration
hint: Create a self-contained training module that lets users practice sorting books by Dewey Decimal or Library of Congress classification codes. The module presents sets of items with call numbers and asks the user to arrange them in correct shelf order. Include streak tracking (e.g., 5 correct in a row triggers a Congratulations state) and feedback on errors. Use local storage for progress to keep it sovereign and offline-capable.

title: Idle-State Terminal Guard Module
input: You are building a bounded Python module for idle-state management on shared/public terminals. The module must: 1) Detect system idle time across Windows/macOS/Linux using platform-specific APIs, 2) Accept configurable idle thresholds (e.g., 5min, 15min), 3) Trigger screensaver, display sleep, or kiosk lock when threshold exceeded, 4) Detect user activity and restore normal state, 5) Run as a local service suitable for kiosk, library, or public terminal deployments. Write only the module code with clear submodule boundaries, no broad UI or cloud features.
hint: Focus on the idle-detection-to-action pipeline, not on screensaver content or visual effects. The module is a utility for terminal administrators, not an end-user application.

title: LocalSherlock - Personal Federated Search Module
input: Search across my local markdown notes, code files, and research documents simultaneously
hint: Focus on unified search across user-owned local collections with click-to-open, not a cloud search service

title: MathSprint Arcade
input: help my child practice arithmetic facts at their current skill level with fun, timed arcade-style rounds where they tap the correct answer from visual options
hint: prioritize answer-target interaction speed, configurable time pressure per difficulty tier, local-only data, no cloud accounts required

title: TextMeasure Utility
input: string: string, fontFamily: string, fontSize: number, fontWeight?: string | number, fontStyle?: string, lineHeight?: number, options?: { containerWidth?: number, exact?: boolean }
hint: Create a text measurement utility that calculates the rendered width (and optionally height) of a string given a font family, size, weight, style, and line height. Use Canvas 2D or DOM-based measurement for precision. Include batch measurement and max-fit font size estimation via binary search.

title: ArchiveInspector
input: archiveFile: string | File | URL, options?: { includeMetadata?: boolean, maxDepth?: number, filterPattern?: string }
hint: A local-first module that accepts an archive file path or remote URL, detects the archive format (ZIP, TAR, 7z, RAR, RO-Crate), parses the internal directory/index structure without performing full decompression, and returns a structured list of contained files with metadata. Supports selective extraction where users choose specific files to unpack. Integrates with file browsers or import workflows as a preview/inspection step.

title: AppShell Bootstrap Kit
input: Create a PySide2 application template with: main window class with standard layout, menu bar factory for adding Apple/File/custom menus, navigation controller that ties arrow keys and menu commands to card/screen changes, progress indicator widget, search bar with callback support, reusable dialog functions (about, quit confirmation), centralized error handler with user-friendly display. Include commented code explaining each component's purpose and how to customize.
hint: Generate a reusable Python PySide2 GUI shell template with main window, menu bar, navigation sidebar, progress indicator, search input, and error dialog handlers - all as importable modules so a developer can fork and customize for their specific internal tool without rebuilding boilerplate.

title: Japanese TTS Converter Module
input: Japanese text to convert to speech
hint: Japanese text input with voice selection and audio playback

title: CountryCurrency Reference Module
input: look up country and currency reference information
hint: Create a local-first sovereign module that provides structured reference data for countries and their monetary units. Include ISO 4217 currency codes, currency names, symbols, and country metadata. Allow browseable country entries with a display setting for preferred currency unit (e.g., ECU vs USD). The module serves the single job of looking up structured country and currency information.

title: Personal Vehicle Maintenance Log
input: vehicle make, model, year, VIN, license plate, current odometer
hint: add a maintenance record entry with service date, odometer, service type, cost, provider, notes, and next due mileage/date

title: Binary Assessment Scorecard
input: What is the assessment name, what questions should be asked (each expecting yes/no or pass/fail), and what pass threshold (e.g., 8 out of 10 passes)?
hint: Collect assessment title, add binary questions, set pass threshold, present questions sequentially or in batch, show running count as responses are captured, compute final score, render pass or fail based on threshold

title: QuickStore Lite
input: An embedded storefront creation wizard for solo sellers. The user has a simple product or service to sell. They don't want to configure a merchant account or manage hosting. They need: 1) A guided onboarding flow that captures basic seller info, 2) A simple hosted product/catalog manager, 3) Built-in payment processing that handles the merchant relationship (like Stripe Checkout or Square), 4) A minimal customizable storefront, and 5) Basic order tracking. The system handles all payment infrastructure. Design a bounded module that walks a non-technical user through launching their store in under 10 minutes.
hint: A wizard-based store builder for solo sellers who need a hosted storefront with built-in payment processing, no separate merchant account required.

title: Annual Tomato War Festival Landing Page
input: Design a single-page event landing site for an annual community food fight festival called 'Tomato War' that has been running for decades. The page should include: event title and date prominently displayed, a history section explaining the tradition and its origins, a visual gallery of past event photos, clear call-to-action to RSVP with options to join as participant or spectator, a registration form collecting name/email/role preference, and organizer contact information. Keep it playful and community-focused.
hint: single-page event landing page with history section, photo gallery, and RSVP registration form

title: IT Consultant Directory & Matching Module
input: user needs an IT consultant with expertise in [specialty] located in [region] for [purpose: procurement advice|training|implementation|custom development]
hint: Bounded to consultant discovery and initial contact workflow, not full project management or vendor contract execution

title: Internal Link Insertion Utility
input: What target record or page should this link connect to?
hint: Select from existing records or paste a target URL

title: ProfilePage
input: Build a bounded single-page interactive resume/profile viewer module. It should have navigation between 6 sections: Objectives, Education, Capabilities (skills), Experience, Personal, and References. Each section displays relevant content. The design should be clean, professional, and suitable for presenting to hiring managers or for networking. No backend required - static HTML/CSS/JS only.
hint: Create a single-page interactive resume module with tabbed section navigation. Include: objectives summary, education history, skills/capabilities grid, experience timeline, personal details, and references. Use clean, professional styling with smooth transitions between sections. Keep it local-first with no external dependencies.

title: Genealogy Tree Navigator
input: Implement a genealogy visualization module with these requirements: 1) Accept GEDCOM file upload and parse into internal model 2) Render interactive SVG tree showing multi-generational relationships with zoom/pan/collapse controls 3) Display person detail panel with editable fields and relationship lists 4) Maintain parent-child and spouse relationship graph in memory 5) Persist loaded tree to IndexedDB for session continuity 6) Export modified data back to GEDCOM format 7) All processing client-side for privacy 8) React + Vite stack preferred 9) Clean modular architecture separating parser, visualization, and data layers
hint: Build a bounded, privacy-first genealogy module: single-user local-first application that parses GEDCOM files, renders interactive multi-generational tree visualization with zoom/pan, allows viewing and editing person records including relationships (parents, children, spouses), stores data in browser IndexedDB, and exports modified GEDCOM. No cloud sync, no collaboration features - pure local sovereignty.

title: Physical Media Label Designer
input: user provides: media type (cassette/CD/vinyl/storage), label content (title, subtitle, metadata), optional reference image or graphic, target paper size
hint: supports common physical media label dimensions (cassette spine/j-card, CD jewel case, vinyl label, archive box)

title: Release Artifact Sanitizer
input: project source directory or template bundle with mixed debug/production code and UI elements
hint: A CLI tool or build plugin that scans source files for debug statements (console.log, print statements, logging calls), dev-only UI components (debug buttons, test fields, development-only panels), and configuration flags, then applies configurable removal/hide/lock rules to produce a sanitized production build. Integrates with webpack, rollup, esbuild, or low-code platform build pipelines.

title: Fingerspelling Practice Module
input: Build a focused fingerspelling practice module: user types a word or selects from vocabulary categories, then watches sequential ASL fingerspelling animation at adjustable speed with audio feedback. Child-friendly but suitable for adult learners. Approximately 200 word library expandable. Local-first with optional sync.
hint: Emphasize clear letter-shape visibility over flash-card style letter display. Prioritize smooth video transitions between letters. Include beginner/speed settings. Target: ASL learners working on receptive fingerspelling skills.
patternFamily: educational drill and interactive language practice

title: Fantasy Baseball Team Tracker
input: Create a bounded local-first spreadsheet (Excel/Sheets/LibreOffice Calc) for tracking a single fantasy baseball team's roster and stats. The module should include: (1) Roster sheet with player name, position, active/reserve status, and acquisition date; (2) Weekly stat logging sheet for recording batting and pitching stats from newspaper box scores; (3) Transaction log for releases, reactivations, and reservations with dates; (4) Simple trend visualization of key player stats over time; (5) Trade planning worksheet with side-by-side player comparison and basic projection notes. No cloud features, no league integration—purely personal team tracking. Format should be printable and work fully offline.
hint: Local-first spreadsheet template for tracking one fantasy baseball team's roster moves and weekly stats from newspaper box scores, with transaction logging and trade-planning views.

title: New Jersey Local Reference Module
input: User seeks to retrieve factual information about New Jersey localities including cities, towns, school districts, parks, and basic reference data within a specific geographic region
hint: Bounded to single-state regional reference, not national directory

title: PrintJobConfigurationModal
input: callingContext: object, initialValues: PrintJobConfig | null, allowedRanges: PageRangeConstraints | null
hint: Present modal form capturing copies (1-N), page range (startPage, endPage with validation), collation preference, and output format. Return structured PrintJobConfig: { copies: number, fromPage: number, toPage: number, collate: boolean, outputFormat: 'print' | 'pdf' | 'preview' }. Validate page range against allowedRanges if provided.

title: Local-First Annotated Document Reader
input: I need to read and annotate a long-form document (e-book or PDF). The document is in my local filesystem. I want to navigate via table of contents, search within the text, place bookmarks, and add per-section notes.
hint: Design a local-first bounded module for reading and annotating e-books or document files. It should load documents from local storage, render them for comfortable reading, provide table of contents navigation, full-text search, bookmarking, and inline note-taking. The module runs locally without cloud dependencies.

title: U.S. Military Rank Reference Module
input: rank_lookup_request
hint: Create a bounded module for looking up U.S. military rank hierarchy and insignia across six service branches. Include branch selector, rank tier filtering (enlisted E-1/E-10, warrant W-1/W-5, officer O-1/O-10), visual insignia display, and per-rank detail cards with pay grade and role description. Prioritize offline-first architecture suitable for training environments.

title: BizCard Vault
input: Keep Track of Businesses EASY! - From PeterSoft BcardDB: A lightweight, offline-capable module to capture, store, and search professional contact details from business cards. Core functions: add new contact from manual entry or scanned card image, view full contact record with all fields (name, company, title, phone, email, address, notes), search/filter by name or company, edit existing records, delete/archive contacts. Optional: basic export to CSV or vCard. No cloud sync required. Single-user local-first design.
hint: Maintain a minimal, fast, local-first business card database focused on quick capture and retrieval of professional contacts without requiring cloud sync or complex CRM overhead

title: HTML Minifier Module
input: Create a bounded module that minifies HTML files for production deployment. The module should accept HTML source files, apply configurable minification rules (remove unnecessary whitespace, comments, redundant attributes, optional closing tags), support preservation of code blocks that must remain unminified, and output optimized files with reduced payload size for faster page loading.
hint: Build an HTML minification module that accepts raw HTML input and produces optimized output with configurable options for removing whitespace, comments, optional tags, and preserving specific code blocks. Include CLI and programmatic API entry points for integration into frontend build pipelines.

title: Card Condition Catalog
input: Personal card collector wants to catalog their collection offline with condition grading and value tracking
hint: Local-first trading card collection manager focused on personal inventory and condition-based value estimation - no marketplace, no social features, offline-capable

title: Embedded Script Extractor
input: Create a local-first embedded script extraction tool that reads Office documents (Word, Excel with VBA macros), compiled assemblies (DLL/EXE with embedded resources), and binary formats containing embedded scripts. Export all extracted scripts to readable text files organized by source. Support batch extraction from directories. Output includes source filename, script type, and extracted code. The tool runs offline with no network dependency.
hint: Focus on the extraction task itself. Do not build debugging, analysis, or visualization features. Keep the scope to reading source formats and dumping to text files.

title: Developer FAQ Module
input: Design a bounded FAQ module for developer documentation that allows creating, organizing, and searching question-answer entries by topics, with support for schema markup and links to related resources
hint: Create a developer FAQ module with question-answer entries organized by topics, search functionality, and schema markup support for documentation sites

title: Entomology Collection Directory
input: Create a local-first entomology specimen collection directory module enabling users to find and annotate natural history collections, lending sources, and specialist experts with structured records and personal research notes
hint: The module should support structured collection records, specialist profiles, lending details, searchable taxonomy, and a personal annotation layer. Retain local-first data ownership without requiring cloud sync.

title: folder-ops
input: You are building a local-first folder management utility module. It should provide three bounded actions: create a folder at a given path, delete a folder, and present a folder picker dialog. This module serves application developers who need to manage folders inside their software workflows without relying on cloud APIs or broad file management suites. Use modern JavaScript/TypeScript with async support. Include error handling for common scenarios (path not found, permission denied, folder not empty). Prefer minimal dependencies and cross-platform compatibility.
hint: Create a bounded module that exposes three folder operations: createFolder(path), deleteFolder(path), and chooseFolder(options). Keep it focused on the core job of folder lifecycle and selection within apps—do not drift into file operations, cloud storage, or broad filesystem utilities. Prefer synchronous and async APIs, minimal dependencies, and cross-platform path handling.

title: Internal Tool Scaffold Shell
input: Create a starter shell for a bounded internal workflow tool. Include: main navigation menu with 3 sections, shared layout component, at least 2 navigation paths (primary and alternate), an intake/registration form screen with basic fields, and a toggleable help system accessible from all views. The shell should serve as a bootstrap template for new internal modules.
hint: Generate a bounded internal tool shell with: (1) configurable navigation menu with multiple sections, (2) reusable shared layout wrapper, (3) alternate navigation routes between screens, (4) intake/form screen with validation, (5) contextual help panel toggled from any view. Keep it self-contained and locally-runnable.

title: ZIndexLayerManager
input: Create a bounded module for managing z-index/layer ordering of canvas elements. The module should provide: 1) Bring to Front and Send to Back actions for moving elements to extremes, 2) Raise and Lower actions for single-step movement, 3) A layer panel showing current stacking order, 4) Keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+] to raise, Ctrl+[ to lower, Ctrl+Shift+] to front, Ctrl+Shift+[ to back), 5) Undo/redo support for layer changes, 6) Visual feedback showing element position in stack. Elements can be UI components, shapes, or canvas objects. The module should be selection-aware and update the layer panel when selection changes.
hint: Implement z-order controls for a visual canvas component editor

title: Workspace Compactor
input: list of file paths or directory paths to compact
hint: process each target, apply compression/deduplication, track progress, emit summary

title: TextField Value Transformer Utility Library
input: A developer needs a small utility library to attach to form fields in their low-code builder. They want to normalize user input (trim whitespace, enforce case), apply input masks (phone numbers, dates), and validate values in real-time as the user types.
hint: Generate a TypeScript utility module with named export transformation functions (normalize, format, validate) and a pipeline composer for chaining. Include JSDoc, unit tests skeleton, and CommonJS/ESM dual package exports.

title: HTML Template Compiler
input: HTML file(s)
hint: Target runtime language (JS/TS/other) and module format (ESM/CJS)

title: Decision Table Editor - Local First
input: Create a local-first decision table editor module that allows a single user to define business rules as rows with conditions and conclusions. The module should include: (1) a fact schema editor to define input variables with types, (2) a visual condition builder for creating rule conditions from facts, (3) an action mapper for defining conclusions/outputs, (4) real-time validation to detect incomplete or overlapping rules, (5) a test harness to run facts against the rule set and see derived conclusions, and (6) a compact rule execution engine that evaluates facts against rules in priority order. The tool should support 10-20 rules maximum, run entirely offline, and store rules in a local JSON file. No cloud sync, no multi-user collaboration, no enterprise workflow integration.
hint: Focus on the local-first, single-user, small-scale nature. Emphasize the decision table visualization (rows=rules, columns=conditions+actions) and the test/execute workflow. Avoid drift toward enterprise decision management platforms.

title: StyledSelect - Cached Custom Dropdown Component
input: A React component that renders a custom-styled dropdown select with configurable typography, sizing, and border treatment. The component accepts an options array, selected value, and onChange callback. It supports custom theming via CSS variables or styled-components tokens. Includes a cache hook to preload and store menu options in memory for faster subsequent renders. The trigger button displays the selected value and opens the dropdown on click. The dropdown panel displays options with hover states and keyboard navigation support.
hint: Use react-select or build a custom dropdown with useState for open/close, useMemo for option filtering. Implement useEffect for cache preloading on mount. Style with theme-aware CSS custom properties matching design system tokens for colors, spacing, typography.

title: Loan vs. Investment Comparator
input: a user wants to evaluate whether it makes more sense to pay down a loan early or invest that money instead. The module should accept loan details (principal, interest rate, term) and investment details (starting amount, monthly contribution, expected return, time horizon). It outputs the amortization schedule for the loan showing principal/interest breakdown per payment, the investment projection showing growth over time, and a comparison view that aligns both timelines so the user can see the opportunity cost of allocating extra money to loan payoff vs. investment.
hint: Create a bounded calculator that takes loan terms and investment parameters as separate input forms, computes monthly payment amortization and compound growth projection, then displays results in aligned timelines for direct financial decision-making, with inline educational tips on how amortization and compound interest work

title: LocalRef
input: single user, local-first, SQLite, searchable citation database, offline reference manager, research notes
hint: Design a bounded module for a single-user local reference database where users can store citation records (title, author, year, source, tags, notes), perform fast full-text and fielded search, and manage personal research collections without cloud dependency.

title: Bulk Copyright Notice Updater
input: Select source files, choose copyright template, confirm apply, review audit log
hint: The user selects multiple files, picks a pre-defined copyright notice template (or creates a new one), the system applies it to each file, and records the operation in an immutable audit log that can be exported for compliance reporting.
sourceTitle: Add Copyright Notice to a sta...

title: Chance Poem Studio
input: Build a bounded creative tool called 'Chance Poem Studio' that lets users select pools of words, images, and audio clips, configure randomness parameters, and generate unique multimedia poem combinations. Include a canvas that displays mixed word-image-sound results with remixing controls. Keep it as a single sovereign local-first module focused purely on creative chance generation.
hint: Create a local-first tool for random multimedia poetry generation with configurable pools and creative remix controls

title: Scriptable Music Notation Editor
input: Create a bounded music notation editor module that: 1) Provides a text editor for composing music in LilyPond-style notation or similar scriptable format; 2) Supports save/open of project files to local storage; 3) Includes audio preview/playback of the notation; 4) Enables copying the notation output to clipboard for pasting into external script-based workflows (like HyperCard scripts, Python music libraries, or DAW automation); 5) Works entirely local-first with no cloud dependencies. The module should be a single sovereign tool, not part of a broader suite.
hint: A bounded music notation editor module that lets users compose in text-based notation, save/load projects, preview audio, and export script-ready output to other tools. Local-first, no cloud required.

title: VocabDrill
input: Design VocabDrill as a sovereign, local-first HTML/JS module. It should let users (1) build decks with term/answer cards (front text, back text, optional audio/image), (2) enter quiz mode where cards are shown one at a time, (3) reveal answers on click, (4) mark correct/incorrect, and (5) compute and display a percentage score at session end. Include export/import of decks as JSON for backup. No user accounts, no cloud features.
hint: Create a local-first vocabulary drill module where users create flashcard decks with front/back term pairs, study them with optional spaced repetition, take timed quizzes that auto-check answers, and receive a percentage score summary. No cloud sync required; data stored in browser IndexedDB or local JSON.

title: Quiz-Gated Exploration Tour Module
input: Write a bounded prompt for a quiz-gated exploration module where users progress through a series of rooms/stages by correctly answering contextual questions. Each stage has a scene, an informational prompt bubble, and a question. Correct answers advance to the next stage; incorrect answers offer retry or hint.
hint: Focus on a single training or onboarding use case with 5-10 stages. Each stage: scene view → question bubble → answer input → validation → advance/retry logic. Keep it self-contained, not a general quiz engine.
sourceTitle: DragonQuest II - The Return of the King

title: Thai Script & Pronunciation Primer
input: Create a bounded local-first Thai alphabet training module for English speakers. The module teaches 44 consonants organized by phonetic class, standalone vowels, and tone marks. Each letter displays visual form, romanized name, and native audio pronunciation. Lessons progress from recognition to production. Audio drills present a letter visually with audio; user selects matching letter from options. Quizzes test recognition and pronunciation matching.
hint: Focus on audio-visual letter association, not vocabulary or grammar. No cloud sync, no social features, no ads. Single-user device-bound progress. Scope: alphabet only, not conversational Thai.

title: Script Bundle Inspector
input: Target application bundle or packaged file path
hint: Provide the path or drop a bundled application file (.app, .apk, .exe, .jar, .nupkg, etc.) to extract and inspect its embedded scripts and configuration files.

title: Inuktitut Lessons 7-12 Study Companion
input: Web app module: Inuktitut Lessons 7-12 Vocabulary Study Companion. Features: lesson selector for lessons 7-12; each lesson shows a list of vocabulary words as clickable flashcards. On click, flip to reveal English translation and example sentence. Below each card, two buttons: 'Know' and 'Don't Know' that track progress locally. A progress summary view shows how many words are marked 'known' per lesson. Design: clean, minimal, high contrast for readability. Use localStorage to persist progress between sessions. No server required.
hint: Create a local-first web module with a lesson index for lessons 7-12. Each lesson displays vocabulary flashcards with Inuktitut text, phonetic pronunciation, English translation, and example usage. Include a simple self-assessment drill that tracks known/unknown per word and shows progress across the six lessons. No backend—store state in browser local storage.

title: Spelling Quest: Enchanted Word Hunt
input: Write a guided spelling adventure game where early literacy learners (grades 1-4) navigate a fantasy map, find hidden target words, and spell them correctly to progress, with character selection, hint system, and visual feedback.
hint: Focus on: map-based level navigation, character role selection, spelling input with visual letter feedback, hint reveal mechanics, progress persistence, child-friendly UI with clear success/failure states. Exclude: broad literacy curriculum, social features, analytics dashboards.
sourceTitle: Word Quest (aka The Quest for the Enchanted Word)

title: MenuGate
input: target_menu: str, target_item: str | None = None
hint: Write a Python function that checks if a named menu item exists in the foreground desktop application window. It should accept menu title and item title, return a boolean for use in a conditional automation branch, and handle cases where the menu or item is absent gracefully.

title: Islamic History Reference Module
input: user wants to browse and look up Islamic history in a self-contained offline-capable reference
hint: local-first static reference with timeline navigation

title: Local Music Collection Catalog
input: I want to catalog my personal music collection. I need to store: album title, artist name, track list (text), and personal notes (e.g., where I got it, mood, rating). I want to view my collection sorted by artist or title, and search across all fields. I want this to run locally on my machine—no cloud, no internet required.
hint: A single-file local-first module for cataloging personal music. Users add records with title, artist, tracklist, and freeform notes. Views sort by any metadata field. No cloud sync, no streaming—just a private, queryable index of owned music.

title: StateMap Practice
input: Create a focused US state geography labeling practice module for K-12 students. The module should: (1) allow selection of individual states, state groups, or regions (Northeast, Southeast, etc.); (2) display a blank US map with clickable/draggable state boundaries; (3) provide context-sensitive hints or clues for each state; (4) enable learners to place state labels via click or drag; (5) provide immediate visual feedback on correct/incorrect placements; (6) track completion status and accuracy. Keep it bounded to US state geography only—not full world geography or comprehensive curriculum.
hint: constrain to US states only; single-page interaction model; offline-capable for classroom use

title: DaylightProbe
input: Local sunrise/sunset calculator that computes exact sunrise, sunset, solar noon, and total daylight duration for any user-selected date and saved location, with full CRUD for a personal location database.
hint: Focus on precise calculation accuracy using the SunCalc library and a minimal local-first persistence layer for user locations. Avoid cloud sync, broad weather data, or unnecessary features.

title: Ion Converter - Educational Chemistry Reference Tool
input: You are a compact ion name↔formula converter tool with a local knowledge base of common polyatomic ions and simple ions. When given an input string, auto-detect whether it is an ion name (e.g., 'sulfate') or ion formula (e.g., 'SO4^2-'). Return the converted form: if name provided, output the chemical formula with charge; if formula provided, output the ion name. Include basic validation (e.g., charge balance check hint). Keep it bounded: focus on ion conversion only, not full compound synthesis. Input: [USER_PROVIDED_ION_STRING]. Output the converted ion name or formula in a clear format.
hint: Use compact ion database (~50-100 common ions), simple regex for input type detection, pattern-based fallback for basic ion formulas (e.g., Cl- → chloride), avoid expanding to broad compound chemistry.

title: Local IRC Client
input: Design and implement a lightweight, local-first IRC client module that enables users to join live text conversations across multiple channels and exchange messages in real time. The module should provide a channel list for managing multiple conversations, a message timeline for viewing message history, text input for composing and sending messages, and connection/status controls for managing server connections. Prioritize minimal dependencies and local data storage.
hint: Consider WeeChat, HexChat, or a custom minimal IRC implementation as reference points. Ensure the module handles connection management, message parsing, channel switching, and basic text formatting while maintaining a small footprint.

title: TeacherGrade Pro
input: Teacher records student grades for an assignment, specifying weighting rules and mark type (points or percentage)
hint: Weighted average calculation, curve adjustment, student progress report generation with class averages

title: UI Button Asset Library Module
input: A designer's workflow of browsing button style options and saving favorites to a personal collection
hint: <invoke>DesignTool | browseButtonGallery | filter='{style:3d shadow,category:windows3x-like}'</invoke> <invoke>DesignTool | previewButtonAsset | assetId='btn_048' | states=['default','hover','pressed']</invoke> <invoke>Scrapbook | collectAsset | assetId='btn_048' | destinationFolder='currentProject'</invoke>

title: Technical Writing Style Guide Trainer
input: Create a bounded, local-first technical writing style guide training module that teaches users how to apply consistent writing conventions. The module should include: 1) Explanations of core style guide concepts from established guides (Google Developer Documentation Style Guide, GitLab Technical Writing Fundamentals, DigitalOcean conventions); 2) Interactive exercises where users apply specific rules to sample technical content; 3) Practice checkpoints that assess ability to identify and correct convention violations; 4) A reference card summarizing key rules for quick lookup. Focus on practical application rather than comprehensive theory. Module should be self-contained and work offline.
hint: Avoid creating a comprehensive documentation suite or broad writing platform. Keep the module focused on teaching specific style guide application through practice, not on serving as a reference library or content management system.

title: Shareware Registrant Ledger
input: Create a minimal local-first module for tracking software registrants. Each record includes: name, email, license key, registration date, status (active/expired/trial), purchase amount, and notes. Provide basic CRUD operations and simple search/filter by status or date range. No enterprise features - just a simple ledger for a small developer.
hint: Prefer SQLite or a simple JSON-based local store. Keep the schema flat. No authentication required if running locally. Focus on the core lookup and admin tasks.

title: DrawKit: Lightweight Canvas Drawing Primitives
input: Build a minimal npm package of reusable canvas drawing utilities for custom app UI graphics. Include common shapes (rect, circle, polygon), path drawing helpers, and fill/stroke configuration. No framework dependencies, just pure functions wrapping Canvas2D API.
hint: Focus on the most common 80% of drawing operations developers need, avoid feature creep like filters or animations - those belong in separate modules

title: Memory Benchmark Module
input: User initiates memory assessment session
hint: A module that presents sequential memory exercises, scores each independently, calculates a composite score (0-100), and provides a performance rating summary. All processing local-first. Not a clinical diagnostic tool.

title: PlainText Publisher
input: A non-technical user provides a plain-text or lightweight-markup document and needs to generate clean, styled HTML without touching code
hint: focus on format-agnostic conversion, template selection, and one-click export rather than editor features

title: Extension API Inspector and Interceptor
input: Load a target Chrome extension in instrumented mode, capture all API calls it makes to the Chrome extension runtime, display call trace with parameters and context in a developer panel, allow selection of any captured call to set a custom return value or conditional override, persist interception rules for reuse in test scenarios
hint: Focus on message passing and chrome.* APIs rather than network requests

title: WindowPark Toggle
input: Create a bounded module that provides a single global hotkey to toggle the currently active window between its normal position and a parked position at the bottom of the primary screen, preserving the window's exact position and size for instant restoration. The module should be triggered by a configurable modifier+key combination (default: Shift+Ctrl+W), work across all applications, and maintain position history in local storage for instant restore.
hint: Does the module focus solely on a single window toggle function? Does it preserve context without closing the application? Is the trigger mechanism global and non-intrusive?

title: TurboMath Expression Engine
input: Create a high-performance expression evaluation module that computes mathematical formulas across data arrays. The module takes a formula (as a string or AST) and input data arrays, then evaluates the formula for each row/element efficiently using vectorized operations or JIT compilation to achieve 10-20x speedup over naive row-by-row evaluation.
hint: Include: formula grammar/parsing, vectorized evaluation over arrays, built-in math functions, batch processing interface.

title: Scripting Dictionary Explorer & Automation Test Harness
input: You are a macOS automation toolkit that helps developers explore and test inter-application scripting capabilities. Given an input specifying a target application name or scripting task, you should load that application's Scripting Dictionary (sdef), allow the user to browse its suites and commands, help construct valid AppleScript or JXA commands with proper object specifiers and parameters, execute those commands in a sandboxed context, and present structured results or error messages. The module maintains a local library of tested scripts organized by target application and use case.
hint: The bounded module focuses specifically on macOS application automation exploration and testing—not generic workflow automation or web API orchestration. It preserves the HyperTalk-era workflow of reading aete resources (modern sdef) to discover what commands an application supports, then constructing and testing those commands in context. The primary interface is a local macOS utility similar to Script Editor but specialized for dictionary exploration and command testing, with a script library for persisting working automation snippets.

title: Video Widget Generator
input: video source, playback config (autoplay, loop, muted), dimensions/responsiveness, styling preferences
hint: You are a tool that generates configurable video embed widgets. Given a video source URL and desired playback behaviors, produce a ready-to-use video component or embed snippet with the specified configuration (dimensions, controls, autoplay, loop, etc.).

title: Spelling Practice Studio
input: teacher spelling practice custom word lists printable study sheets grade-level
hint: Create a local-first spelling practice module with: (1) pre-loaded leveled word bank spanning elementary to advanced, (2) teacher custom list builder, (3) practice session player that presents words in context sentences, (4) study sheet generator producing printable PDF worksheets, (5) basic progress tracking. No cloud sync required.

title: Folder Picker Helper
input: A developer needs to select a folder from the native file system dialog during an automated workflow or authoring tool operation and get the path back as a string for subsequent file operations
hint: Create a small npm module that presents a native folder picker dialog and returns the selected folder path string to a callback or Promise, supporting Windows/macOS/Linux in a desktop or local-first context

title: Arbitrary Shape Hotspot Component Module
input: Create a bounded component module that renders arbitrary SVG shapes as clickable hotspots, supports point-list or path definition, provides hit-testing, and integrates with a visual canvas editor for placement and action configuration
hint: Focus on the core hit-test mechanism and shape rendering, not full visual editor
sourceTitle: Tricks of the HyperTalk Masters: Polybuttons 1.0

title: Legacy Delimited File Import Module
input: Design a bounded module for importing legacy delimited text files (CSV, TSV, byte-delimited) into a modern application. The module should: (1) accept file upload or path selection, (2) auto-detect delimiter, encoding, and column structure, (3) present a field mapping UI to map source columns to destination schema fields, (4) show a preview of transformed records before commit, (5) execute the import with progress indication, (6) report success count, skip count, and actionable error details. Target users are data migrators and system administrators moving data from legacy databases.
hint: Focus on bounded import workflow: file selection → auto-detection → mapping → preview → execution → reporting. Keep scope tight to delimited file import only, not general ETL or API integration.

title: American English Style Reference Module
input: Query American English style rule or convention
hint: User needs to verify a grammar rule, usage question, or formatting convention in American English

title: PronunciationTutor Module
input: User wants to practice English pronunciation
hint: Create a bounded pronunciation-tutoring module that plays a target phrase via text-to-speech and native audio, prompts the user to repeat it, captures their speech, scores pronunciation quality against the native reference, tracks progress through a curriculum of pronunciation units, and surfaces weak areas for repeated practice.

title: Local Recipe Keeper
input: design a local-first personal recipe database with recipe creation, ingredient management, and meal categorization for a single desktop user
hint: Build a local-first recipe management module

title: Styled Text Format Converter Module
input: Convert rich text between internal styled-text representation and portable RTF/HTML formats while preserving all formatting attributes including fonts, colors, styles, and layout
hint: Focus on bidirectional conversion with style preservation rules, handling edge cases like unsupported formats and style mapping between source and target representations

title: On-Demand Network Share Connector
input: Target network share (UNC path or server/share), optional drive letter, optional credentials, force flag for stuck mappings
hint: Output: Mount success with assigned drive letter OR disconnect confirmation OR error with remediation (admin required, zombie detected)

title: Embedded Audio Playback Module for Authoring Tools
input: What is the core user job to be solved?
hint: Create a composable audio playback component that allows course authors to select, embed, and control background/reference audio tracks within an interactive presentation or e-learning module, exposing play/pause/seek/track-select API to the host authoring environment.

title: Viewport Edge Auto-Scroll Module
input: context: dragState, pointerPosition, viewportBounds, contentBounds, elapsedTime
hint: Implement edge-triggered auto-scroll that monitors pointer proximity to viewport boundaries during drag operations, calculating progressive scroll speed based on distance from edge, and animating smooth pan for oversized canvas navigation.

title: Literary Study Reader
input: Literary work selection and study goals
hint: Enables close reading of canonical texts with integrated scholarly annotations and contextual study notes. Supports segmented navigation (acts, scenes, passages), layered annotation display, and reference linking. NOT a broad DH suite or generic ebook reader - focuses specifically on annotated literary study.

title: Local-First Document Formatting Bridge Module
input: sourceFormat (string), targetFormat (string), content (styled text object)
hint: You are a document formatting utility module. Your bounded responsibility is parsing, serializing, and converting rich text between an application's internal format and external document standards (RTF, HTML, Markdown, Word, PDF). Work entirely offline. Accept styled text input, detect target format, and produce compatible output. Preserve formatting fidelity during conversion. Do not provide UI or editing capabilities—only format transformation.

title: GeoCoord Transformer
input: Build a bounded coordinate conversion module that: (1) Accepts latitude/longitude input in decimal degrees or DMS format, (2) Converts to UTM with automatic zone and hemisphere detection using WGS84 datum, (3) Performs reverse conversion from UTM easting/northing to lat/long, (4) Validates coordinate ranges and displays clear error messages for invalid inputs, (5) Provides one-click copy of converted values, (6) Is scoped as a single-purpose utility suitable for local-first deployment
hint: Convert coordinates between latitude/longitude and UTM formats for GIS and field mapping workflows

title: TrigFunctionPlotter
