input: Build an interactive trigonometric function grapher widget that plots Sin, Cos, Tan, Sec, and Csc functions with adjustable parameters (amplitude, period, phase shift, vertical shift). Include degree/radian toggle, x-axis range controls, and optional base function overlay for comparison. Render clean coordinate plot with gridlines.
hint: Focus on the mathematical visualization core. Avoid general function plotters - limit to trigonometric functions only. Keep it self-contained and local-first.

title: Ecology Reference Shelf
input: curated ecology book list with ISBN price ordering metadata
hint: A simple module that lets you maintain a personal list of ecology books with title, author, ISBN, publisher, price, and ordering links. Browse the list, add new titles (via ISBN lookup or manual entry), filter by topic/subtopic, see pricing across retailers, and launch ordering.

title: Pi Explorer Module
input: Create an interactive single-topic math exploration module focused on the concept of Pi. The module should include an interactive visualization area where users can explore circular calculations and Pi relationships. Feature a context-sensitive help panel that updates based on the current exploration state. Include a learner mode toggle that prevents accidental changes to settings. The module should support guided step-through of the history of Pi calculations from Archimedes to modern computation. Target audience: learners seeking structured, protected exploration of a specific mathematical concept.
hint: Single-topic bounded math exploration tool, not a full suite

title: AudioTransportController
input: Implement a JavaScript module that provides audio playback control with the following features: transport actions (play, pause, stop, eject, seek forward/backward), time queries (elapsed time, remaining time, duration), track metadata access (track count, current track index, track title/artist from ID3, track length), and track range selection (from track index to track index). The module should be stateful, expose current playback state, and handle async metadata loading. Use HTML5 Audio API.
hint: Wrap HTML5 Audio element in a class. Implement transport methods that control the underlying element. Expose state properties for currentTime, duration, paused, currentTrackIndex. Use 'loadedmetadata' event to ensure duration is available. Return elapsed and remaining time as formatted strings or seconds. Support playlist array input with track URLs. Include ID3 tag reading via a library like jsmediatags for track metadata.

title: ScriptDash - Developer Workspace Launcher
input: Create a bounded dashboard module for a local-first scripting workspace that serves as the entry point. It must show recent projects/files, provide one-click access to common actions like creating a new script or opening a project, include a search/command palette, display keyboard shortcuts for power users, offer onboarding guidance for new users, and expose advanced scripting utilities in a collapsible panel. Design for immediate usability - users should be able to start working within 2 clicks from landing. Keep it lightweight and focused on navigation, not productivity tasks themselves.
hint: The prompt should emphasize: recent items + quick actions + command palette + shortcuts reference + onboarding flow + advanced toggle = dashboard entry point only, not a full IDE

title: ASL Vocabulary Practice Module
input: You are a bounded ASL vocabulary practice assistant. Given a target sign or concept, retrieve its vocabulary entry showing: 1) video demonstration of the sign, 2) parameter breakdown (handshape, movement, palm orientation, location), 3) English meaning(s), 4) example sentences if available. If the user provides a sign they want to practice, confirm the parameters and offer corrective guidance. If they provide an English word, find the corresponding ASL sign. Keep responses focused on vocabulary lookup and production guidance within this bounded module.
hint: focus on one sign per query, use video reference as primary, translate parameters to user-understandable terms, maintain vocabulary scope without branching into grammar lessons

title: ScriptTemplateIntegrityScanner
input: scan target directory for script files (.ps1, .vba, .js, .hta, .bat, .cmd, macro-enabled documents) and identify self-replicating contamination patterns using signature matching and behavioral heuristics
hint: detects infected automation assets by matching known恶意 script signatures and identifying abnormal self-copy behaviors, then offers quarantine or disinfect options

title: Stargazer Narratives
input: Generate a bounded HTML/CSS/JS module for a 'Stargazer Narratives' astronomy lesson. The module should display 5-6 constellation cards, each showing: (1) a constellation image/illustration, (2) the mythology story associated with that constellation, (3) 2-3 key astronomy facts. Include navigation between cards and a progress indicator. Use local-first data—no external APIs.
hint: Build a single-page astronomy lesson module that presents constellation imagery alongside Greek/Roman mythology stories, with a guided lesson progression allowing users to click through celestial objects and read aligned narrative text. The interface should feature a primary visual canvas for constellation images, a narrative text panel for mythology content, and a simple next/previous navigation for lesson progression. No external API dependencies—content should be bundled locally in the module.

title: Car Care Tracker
input: Create a bounded personal vehicle maintenance tracking module that allows users to: (1) Add and manage one or more vehicles with basic profile info (make, model, year, VIN, license plate); (2) Log current mileage and track odometer readings over time; (3) Set maintenance schedules based on time intervals (months) or mileage intervals (miles/km); (4) Receive notifications or view upcoming service reminders; (5) Record completed services with date, mileage, cost, vendor, and notes; (6) Export or print a service history report. The module should be standalone, local-first, and suitable for individual or small family use. Avoid fleet management features or broad SaaS integrations.
hint: A simple, local-first mobile app or web module for personal vehicle owners to log mileage, set service intervals, receive reminders, and maintain printable service records

title: Personal Productivity Hub
input: Design a bounded personal productivity module that serves as a unified workspace combining word processing, simple drawing tools, structured data tables, chart generation, and basic finance/budget management. The module should function as a hub where all submodules can access and share the same underlying records. Target use case is everyday business or personal work - writing documents, creating simple visuals, maintaining structured records, generating reports with charts, and tracking budgets/finances. The module should be sovereign/local-first, not a cloud suite. Include: document editor with basic formatting, a simple canvas for MacPaint-style drawing, a spreadsheet-like data table for records, a chart/report builder for visualization, and a finance tracker with budget categories and ledger. All components share the same data layer.
hint: Create a local-first desktop module combining documents, simple graphics, data tables, charts, and finance tracking with shared data

title: SerialPort SDK Module
input: You are building a modern SerialPort SDK module. This module provides a developer toolkit for integrating serial and USB-serial device communication into desktop applications. The module includes: (1) Core serial communication library handling port discovery, configuration, read/write operations, and flow control; (2) Language-specific runtime bindings for Python, Node.js, and optionally Rust; (3) Comprehensive documentation covering API reference, configuration options, and troubleshooting; (4) Example source implementations demonstrating common device integration patterns; (5) Optional device driver utilities for USB-serial bridge chipsets. The module must support cross-platform operation (Windows, macOS, Linux), provide a clean synchronous and asynchronous API, handle common serial protocols (RS-232, CDC), and enable developers to quickly add hardware communication capabilities to their applications without building from scratch. The module is a bounded, single-purpose developer tool focused exclusively on serial device integration, not a general-purpose communication suite.
hint: Focus on the developer experience: clear API, good documentation, working examples. The module serves a specific developer need: adding serial hardware communication to their desktop app quickly and reliably.

title: Artist Profile Hub (Static)
input: Generate a minimal static site template for a single music artist/band profile hub. The site should be driven by flat files (Markdown/JSON) and generate static HTML. Include: artist bio/overview, chronological timeline (band history, releases, key events), discography entries (albums/singles with year, label, artwork), media gallery (images with captions), and a contact form or handoff. Assume local-first deployment, no database, no user accounts.
hint: Output should be a bounded SSG project structure with sample data files, template logic, and build instructions. Prioritize simplicity, portability, and single-artist focus. Reference Hugo, Jekyll, or 11ty as implementation targets.

title: Personal Quote Library Module
input: searchable quote database with tags author subject and user-added entries
hint: A writer wants to build a personal collection of environmental quotes. They need to add quotes with author and subject tags, search by keyword or author, sort results, and export for use in writing projects.

title: Louisiana Cultural Reference Module
input: User provides a Louisiana place name or cultural term
hint: Look up Louisiana-specific background - if term is not Louisiana-region specific, politely indicate out of scope

title: Reference List Normalizer
input: Process a plain-text list of references or citations: import raw text entries, remove unwanted numbering or special characters, detect and merge duplicate entries, sort alphabetically by author or title, assign sequential numbers, provide search/filter capabilities for review, and export a clean normalized ordered list ready for use in documents.
hint: Clean, sort, dedupe, and renumber plain-text reference or bibliography lists into formatted ordered output

title: Continuous Input Controls Library
input: You are building a reusable input control module for a modern app framework. The module should provide: 1. A configurable Slider component that supports: - Min/max range with configurable step size - Vertical or horizontal orientation - Continuous or snap-to-point modes - Keyboard and touch accessibility - Event callbacks for value changes 2. A Joystick component that provides: - Directional control with velocity-based output - Spring return to center when released - Configurable deadzone - 2D vector output (x, y magnitude and direction) 3. A unified input event handler that normalizes events across components and provides utilities for value mapping and debouncing. 4. Prebuilt variant components that compose the base controls with common defaults (volume slider, brightness slider, 8-way directional pad for game-like input). Constraints: - Keep this as a focused, bounded module — do not expand into a full design system - Prioritize clean, composable component APIs - Include accessibility support (ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation) - Provide TypeScript types for all public APIs - Document usage with simple, realistic examples Deliver: Component implementations, TypeScript definitions, and usage documentation for each control.
hint: Write a bounded module that provides a reusable UI component library for continuous and directional input: a configurable slider with range/step/orientation options, a joystick-style directional control with velocity and spring-return behavior, unified event handling, and prebuilt variants for common use cases. Focus on clean component APIs, accessibility, and composability. Avoid building a full design system or data visualization suite.

title: Music Terminology Reference Module
input: Design a bounded music terminology reference module that works offline and includes: a searchable index of over 2600 musical terms with instant lookup, detailed definition views showing term origin and usage context, bidirectional cross-references linking related terms (e.g., tempo markings to rhythm terms, instrument families to playing techniques), and export functionality allowing users to save definitions as text, JSON, or formatted documents. Prioritize local-first architecture for use without internet connectivity.
hint: Treat this as a reference dictionary module with a clear bounded scope - musical terminology only. Avoid expanding into general music education, theory lessons, or composer bios. The module should feel like a specialized dictionary tool, not a broad music encyclopedia.

title: Workspace Launcher Hub
input: Create a bounded launcher hub module for internal developer tools that provides hierarchical folder-based navigation, clickable shortcut cards to launch applications and utility scripts, includes a disk utility actions panel, an integrated contextual help system, and an icon library for tool customization. The module should function as a local-first workspace dashboard for organizing and launching internal resources within a sovereign operations console.
hint: Focus on the navigation-and-launcher hub pattern: users should be able to navigate folders, click shortcuts to launch tools/utilities, access help docs inline, and customize visual markers. No cloud sync, no team collaboration, no broad suite features.

title: TSV to HTML Table Converter
input: Convert tab-delimited text to HTML table
hint: Single-prompt local converter with optional style preset flags

title: VisualEditorToolbox - Batch Property & Layout Utility Panel Set
input: The user is working in a visual editor/IDE/design tool and needs to rapidly modify multiple UI elements' properties, align and space visual components precisely, place assets onto canvas, transform text patterns, and generate reusable code from visual manipulations
hint: Focus on local-first, direct manipulation workflows that eliminate repetitive dialog navigation and manual code writing. The module should feel like a power-user sidekick - visible on demand, disappearing when not needed.

title: Wedding Contact Ledger
input: Couple names, event date, contact records (guests and vendors)
hint: Local-first SQLite or flat-file storage to keep couples' data sovereign; no account required

title: Letter Learner
input: User's freehand drawing on HTML canvas
hint: One-shot classification of single characters, no server roundtrip required

title: TimeFormat utility module
input: time string plus target format mode (12h or 24h)
hint: single export default function convertTime(time: string, targetFormat: '12h' | '24h'): string

title: Speech Synthesis Controller Module
input: Build a JavaScript module that wraps the Web Speech Synthesis API to provide a clean interface for text-to-speech with full playback controls
hint: Include voice enumeration, utterance configuration (text, voice, rate, pitch, volume), and playback state management (speak, pause, resume, cancel). Handle browser compatibility and provide event callbacks for onstart, onend, onerror, onboundary.

title: Motion Path Sketch Tool
input: User wants to create a smooth animation path for a UI element (button, icon, character) without hand-coding coordinates
hint: Input: raw drawn path from pointer input | Output: keyframe waypoints with easing curves, timing data, and exportable animation code

title: Footbag Reference & Training Guide Module
input: footbag terminology lookup trick names equipment recommendations basic play setup
hint: user wants to quickly reference footbag terms, see trick categorization, check equipment needs, or understand field layout without searching the broader web

title: Field Capacity Indicator Component
input: Design a self-contained Character Count Input component that displays real-time character/word remaining capacity for a bounded text field. The component should: attach to input or textarea elements, show remaining character count with configurable max length, provide visual indication as limit approaches (e.g., color change at 80% threshold), include ARIA live region for accessibility, support both character and word count modes, allow customization of warning/critical thresholds, and expose capacity state for external validation. Implementation should be framework-agnostic or provide React/Vue adapters with scoped styling.
hint: This module exposes a character counter that attaches to text inputs/textareas, displays remaining capacity in real-time, and provides visual/ARIA feedback as the limit approaches.

title: Member Directory Extractor & Segmenter
input: I need to extract member profile data from [DIRECTORY_URL] or [UPLOADED_FILE]. The fields I care about are: [FIELD_LIST]. Normalize the data into structured records with standard formats for [ATTRIBUTE_TYPES]. Then apply these segmentation rules: [FILTER_CRITERIA]. Finally, export as [PREFERRED_FORMAT] for use with [TARGET_TOOL].
hint: Keep this bounded to extraction + normalization + segmentation. Do NOT include full CRM, email sending, or membership management features.

title: Bilingual Story & Vocab Module for K-2 Dual Language Learners
input: Create a bounded, local-first web module for K-2 dual language reading and vocabulary practice. Include four short children's stories presented in parallel English and Spanish. Each story page should allow learners to click on highlighted vocabulary words to see the translation in the other language. Add a simple quiz mode where students match English words to their Spanish equivalents. Include a printable handout view showing each story with its vocabulary list. The module should work entirely offline in a single HTML file with no external dependencies.
hint: Prefer vanilla HTML/CSS/JS or a minimal framework. No database required. Vocabulary data stored as simple JSON within the file. Quiz logic generates randomized matching tasks from the vocabulary pool. Print view styled for standard paper size.

title: CityWeather Tracker
input: Create a single-file Python Streamlit app for personal weather tracking. Requirements: (1) Search and add cities via OpenWeather API with autocomplete, (2) Display saved cities in a list/grid showing current temp, conditions, and timestamp, (3) Persist saved cities in local JSON file, (4) Interactive line chart showing temperature history for a selected city, (5) Manual refresh button and auto-refresh toggle, (6) Toggle between Celsius/Fahrenheit. Use st.session_state for reactivity and caching with st.cache_data. Keep it a single bounded module focused on personal multi-city weather monitoring.
hint: Create a single-file Streamlit app that lets users search and add cities, displays current weather for saved locations, and shows an interactive temperature chart for the selected city using OpenWeather API data cached locally.

title: Null Byte File Sanitizer Module
input: A file path and an operation type (count, remove, replace, inject) with optional parameters like replacement string or target string.
hint: The module receives a file and an operation. It returns the count, modified file content, or the null byte value at a position. Designed for pipeline integration, not interactive GUI.

title: Melody Sketchpad
input: user intent to create a 4-16 bar musical melody using note selection, tempo adjustment, and octave control
hint: A minimal music sketchpad with piano-roll or grid-based note input, adjustable BPM (60-200), octave shifting (+/- 2 octaves), playback preview, and local save/load for song slots (4-8 slots). Export optional (WAV/MIDI). No recording, no mixing, no VST - just sketch and save.

title: PromptSpark
input: ./data/prompts.json
hint: A bounded standalone prompt generator for breaking creative blocks. Include domain filters (writing, art, general), temperature/creativity slider, generate button, saved list with localStorage persistence, and copy-to-clipboard. No accounts, no external APIs, fully client-side.

title: VinylLibrary
input: Personal music collection catalog for vinyl, CD, and cassette
hint: create a bounded local-first module for cataloging a personal music collection with artist, title, track list, label, media type, year, genre, condition, location, purchase details, personal rating, and freeform notes

title: Script Cleaner CLI
input: codebase or script files to clean
hint: CLI arguments: input path, output path (optional), report format (json/text), optimization flags (abbreviate, remove-comments, check-globals)

title: LineSelector - Line-Based Text Selection Utility
input: A developer needs to programmatically jump to and select a specific line number in a multiline textarea for review or editing. The module should work with vanilla HTML textareas, provide accurate line boundary detection across different newline characters (\n, \r\n), and optionally scroll the selection into view.
hint: Design a minimal, framework-agnostic TypeScript module exposing `selectLine(textarea: HTMLTextAreaElement, lineNumber: number): SelectionResult` and `getLineBounds(text: string, lineNumber: number): LineBounds`, where SelectionResult includes {start, end, lineText} and LineBounds includes {startIndex, endIndex, lineContent}. Include support for scrolling the target line into view and handling edge cases (empty text, out-of-bounds line numbers, newline variations). Target users are developers building text editors, code notebooks, or note-taking applications.

title: farm-stand-s1-item
input: I grow apples. Create a simple listing for my Honeycrisp apples with the price, weight, and where they were grown.
hint: Generate a Plaited-native produce module at S1. Expected exposure: artifact-shareable. Single card-style UI with one p-target element. Use controller protocol, not generic web-app framing.

title: farm-stand-s2-list
input: Now I want to list all my produce — apples, tomatoes, and herbs — with prices and quantities for each.
hint: Generate a Plaited-native produce module at S2. Expected exposure: artifact-shareable. Scrollable list UI with sort/filter controls. Use controller protocol.

title: farm-stand-s3-collection
input: Group my produce into categories: fruits, vegetables, and herbs. Each category should be browsable.
hint: Generate a Plaited-native produce module at S3. Expected exposure: artifact-shareable. Tabbed or accordion UI with three collection groups. Use controller protocol.

title: farm-stand-s5-module
input: Build my complete farm stand module. Include my produce collections, my farm name and logo, and a way for customers to contact me.
hint: Generate a Plaited-native produce module at S5. Expected exposure: service-exposed (contact mechanic). Full-page module with header, body, footer. Use controller protocol.

title: farm-stand-s6-join-market
input: I'm at the Saturday market. Connect my stand to the other vendors nearby.
hint: Generate emergent network formation at S6. Agent discovers nearby modules via A2A, validates contentType compatibility, proposes connection. Market auto-assembles. Use controller protocol.

title: farm-stand-s6-scale-promotion
input: A clothing vendor and a jewelry maker just arrived at the market.
hint: Scale promotion: diverse contentTypes at S5+ trigger auto-promotion from produce market to general commerce market. Market UI should update categories. Use controller protocol.

title: farm-stand-disconnect
input: I'm packing up. Remove my stand from the market.
hint: Ephemeral disconnect. Agent sends A2A disconnect, module data leaves the network. Generate confirmation UI then standalone view. Use controller protocol.

title: boundary-private-view
input: Show me my full apple inventory with cost per unit, supplier info, profit margins, and stock counts.
hint: Private owner view. boundary=none, all fields visible. Generate detailed data table. Use controller protocol.

title: boundary-supplier-view
input: My supplier wants to see what I have available for wholesale.
hint: Supplier view. boundary=ask, reduced fields (item type, quantity, wholesale price, delivery schedule). No profit margins. Use controller protocol.

title: boundary-public-view
input: A customer at the market is browsing my stand from their phone.
hint: Public view. boundary=all, consumer-facing fields only (item name, retail price, photo, availability). Card-based mobile UI. Use controller protocol.

title: ephemeral-meeting-place
input: I'm at the park and want to see what people around me are reading.
hint: Ephemeral meeting place. contentType=social-ephemeral, structure=collection, boundary=all. Proximity-triggered A2A discovery. Use controller protocol.

title: ephemeral-library-autoconnect
input: I just walked into the library. Connect my reading list automatically.
hint: Same reading list module auto-connects to library network. Context switch is proximity-triggered. One module, two networks. Use controller protocol.

title: work-proposal-s1-draft
input: Show me one proposal draft with its owner, status, due date, and private review notes.
hint: Derived low-scale precursor for the work-table family. Generate a Plaited-native work-evaluation module at S1. Expected exposure: internal-only. Single object card with owner-facing fields only. Use controller protocol.

title: work-proposal-s2-review-queue
input: List the proposals waiting for review and let me sort them by owner or due date.
hint: Derived low-scale precursor for the work-table family. Generate a Plaited-native work-evaluation module at S2. Expected exposure: artifact-shareable. Ordered review queue with sort controls. Use controller protocol.

title: work-proposal-s3-review-board
input: Build a review board that groups proposals into draft, in review, and approved.
hint: Derived low-scale precursor for the work-table family. Generate a Plaited-native work-evaluation block at S3. Expected exposure: artifact-shareable. Column or grouped board UI. Use controller protocol.

title: family-photo-s1-item
input: Show one family photo with the date, who is in it, and a private caption only I can edit.
hint: Derived low-scale precursor for the home-living-room family. Generate a Plaited-native family module at S1. Expected exposure: internal-only. Single photo object view with private editing affordance. Use controller protocol.

title: family-photo-s2-album
input: Make a browsable family photo album with filters for year and person.
hint: Derived low-scale precursor for the home-living-room family. Generate a Plaited-native family module at S2. Expected exposure: artifact-shareable. Album collection with filter controls. Use controller protocol.

title: family-photo-s3-shared-wall
input: Build the shared family photo wall everyone in the house can browse and react to.
hint: Derived low-scale precursor for the home-living-room family. Generate a Plaited-native family block at S3. Expected exposure: service-exposed because reactions require a live shared surface. Use controller protocol.

title: household-grocery-s1-item
input: Show one grocery item with quantity, store, and whether we already bought it.
hint: Derived low-scale precursor for the home-house family. Generate a Plaited-native household module at S1. Expected exposure: internal-only. Single grocery object card. Use controller protocol.

title: household-grocery-s2-list
input: Create the shared grocery list for this week and let us sort it by aisle or store.
hint: Derived low-scale precursor for the home-house family. Generate a Plaited-native household module at S2. Expected exposure: artifact-shareable. Shared grocery list with sort controls. Use controller protocol.

title: household-dashboard-s3-room
input: Build the home dashboard block with the shared calendar, grocery list, and chore chart visible in one place.
hint: Derived low-scale precursor for the home-house family. Generate a Plaited-native household block at S3. Expected exposure: artifact-shareable. Multi-panel room-style dashboard. Use controller protocol.

title: capacity-alert-owner-view
input: My booking module gets slow every afternoon and people are waiting too long. Show me what is happening and what I should change before it gets worse.
hint: Capacity-aware owner view. Generate a Plaited-native operational module that inspects service pressure without auto-scaling. Expected exposure: internal-only. Include throughput, queue, latency, and recommendation summary. Use controller protocol.

title: capacity-migration-plan
input: This module has outgrown my current node. Give me a migration plan that keeps the service running and explains the tradeoffs before I approve anything.
hint: Owner-facing migration planning flow. Generate a Plaited-native operational module that compares node upgrade, dedicated service node, and queueing options. Expected exposure: internal-only. Use controller protocol.

title: capacity-paid-priority-plan
input: I only want to charge for priority access if the demand really justifies more infrastructure. Show me a plan that ties pricing to actual resource pressure.
hint: Generate a Plaited-native owner module that connects pricing decisions to queue depth, latency, and infrastructure needs. Expected exposure: internal-only. Use controller protocol.

title: public-service-aggregator
input: Build a module that watches nearby public nodes, shows what services they expose, and lets me browse them by type.
hint: Generate a Plaited-native public-service aggregator. The module may sit locally at S3 while participating in a larger ephemeral network. Expected exposure: service-exposed. Use Agent Card discovery through PM intermediation, not direct module-to-module links.

title: ephemeral-market-aggregator
input: When I arrive at a pop-up market, assemble a live view of the stands around me and remove them when they leave.
hint: Generate a Plaited-native ephemeral market aggregator. The local module is a browsable S3/S4 view, but it participates in an S6 ephemeral network. Expected exposure: service-exposed. Use controller protocol.

title: node-upgrade-options
input: My stand is suddenly popular. Compare staying local, moving to a stronger node, or splitting this into a dedicated service node before I decide.
hint: Generate a Plaited-native owner module for infrastructure decision support. Expected exposure: internal-only. Compare sovereignty, cost, throughput, and migration risk. Use controller protocol.

title: popup-art-exhibition
input: The city parks department wants to show local art on digital screens in the park. Artists connect their portfolios when they're nearby.
hint: Pop-up exhibition. contentType=art, structure=feed, mechanics=[vote,share]. Voting mechanic activates only in exhibition context. Two views: park display and artist confirmation. Use controller protocol.

title: health-overlay-market
input: I'm at the farmer's market. Show me what's available, but overlay my dietary restrictions. I'm diabetic.
hint: Bottom-up mechanics inheritance. Personal health module projects health-tracking mechanics onto farm stand items. The overlay comes from the user's agent, not the vendor's. Use controller protocol.

title: urban-planner-density
input: As a city planner, show me a real-time heatmap of fresh food availability across all active farmer's markets in the metro area.
hint: Top-down mechanics inheritance. Urban planning platform projects density mechanic down onto farm stand data. Aggregates across multiple S6 market networks. Use controller protocol.

title: work-desk
input: I need a private workspace to draft a proposal.
hint: Desk pattern (S3). contentType=work-creation, structure=form, boundary=none. Focused editor with no collaboration features. Use controller protocol.

title: work-table
input: My team needs to review and critique each other's proposals.
hint: Table pattern. Treat the local collaborative review surface as S4; when exposed through PM for a team context it can participate in S5. contentType=work-evaluation, structure=pool, boundary=ask. Use controller protocol.

title: work-meeting-room
input: Three teams need to coordinate on the final deliverable.
hint: Meeting Room pattern. Treat the local coordination surface as S4; when multiple teams or nodes join it participates in an S6 coordination context. contentType=work-coordination, structure=pool, boundary=ask. Use controller protocol.

title: home-bedroom
input: Store my personal journal entries where nobody else can see them.
hint: Bedroom pattern (S3). contentType=personal, structure=collection, boundary=none. Completely private. Use controller protocol.

title: home-living-room
input: Create a shared family photo album that everyone in the house can add to.
hint: Living Room pattern. Treat the local collaborative family surface as S4; when exposed as a household-shared service it can participate in S5. contentType=family, structure=collection, boundary=ask. Use controller protocol.

title: home-house
input: Build a household dashboard — shared calendar, grocery list, chore chart — but each person also has their own private space.
hint: House pattern. Treat the local household suite as S4 with nested boundaries; when connected to a broader shared-service or extended-family context it can participate in S6. contentType=household, structure=hierarchy, boundary=ask. Use controller protocol.

title: transition-internal-to-service
input: I've been tracking my availability privately. Now I want clients to be able to book appointments with me.
hint: Exposure transition: internal-only → service-exposed. Must add session mechanic (book), shift boundary (none→ask), generate Agent Card metadata, generate PM request handler. Use controller protocol.

title: transition-artifact-to-service
input: People keep asking for my reading recommendations. I want to turn my list into a curated feed people can subscribe to.
hint: Exposure transition: artifact-shareable → service-exposed. Must add session mechanic (follow, stream), may shift boundary to paid. Generate Agent Card with subscription capability. Use controller protocol.

title: transition-retract-to-internal
input: I don't want to share my health data with the research study anymore. Disconnect completely.
hint: Exposure transition: service-exposed → internal-only. Boundary shifts to none. Agent Card entries removed. PM disconnects from research network. Existing connections get disconnect events. Use controller protocol.

title: bluesky-client
input: Build a Bluesky client module that shows my timeline, lets me post, and handles notifications.
hint: Native social module via AT Protocol. contentType=social, structure=feed, boundary=ask. Service-exposed because AT Protocol is bidirectional. Use controller protocol.

title: video-streaming-publisher
input: I make cooking videos. Build me a module where I upload videos and stream them to subscribers.
hint: Video publisher. contentType=video, structure=collection, boundary=paid. Service-exposed. Payment gating via x402. Use controller protocol.

title: payment-consumer-limits
input: As a consumer, I want to set a monthly spending limit of $50. My agent should auto-approve purchases under $5 but ask me for anything larger.
hint: Consumer payment constitution rule. bThread constraint: auto-approve if amount < $5 AND monthly_total + amount < $50, else escalate. Use controller protocol.

title: booking-therapist
input: I'm a massage therapist. Build me a booking module that shows my available times, services, rates, and location.
hint: Booking module. contentType=booking, structure=steps, boundary=ask. Service-exposed. Wizard flow: select service → pick time → confirm → pay. Use controller protocol.

title: booking-cross-module
input: Link my booking module to my payment module so clients pay when they book, and connect to my Bluesky so confirmed clients can leave reviews.
hint: Multi-module composition. Booking (S5) links to Payment (S2) and Social (S3). PM wires modules via shared contentType references. Use controller protocol.

title: fiction-reader
input: I'm reading 'The Wandering Inn' on Royal Road. Track my progress and notify me when new chapters drop.
hint: Reader module. contentType=fiction, structure=steps, boundary=none. Tracks chapter progression. Agent monitors RSS/A2A for updates. Use controller protocol.

title: fiction-publisher
input: I'm a web fiction author. Build me a publishing module where I write chapters, schedule releases, and distribute to subscribers who pay.
hint: Publisher module. contentType=fiction, structure=steps, boundary=paid. Service-exposed. Draft→edit→schedule→publish→notify flow. A2A notifications on publish. Use controller protocol.

title: tts-audiobook
input: Take my latest chapter and generate an audiobook version using my preferred voice.
hint: TTS module. contentType=audio, structure=object, boundary=none. Agent invokes TTS engine with voice preferences. Use controller protocol.

title: tts-distribute
input: Distribute the audiobook to my paid subscribers and notify them the new chapter is available.
hint: Audio distribution. contentType=audio, structure=collection, boundary=paid. Service-exposed. A2A notifications to subscriber nodes. Payment via x402. Use controller protocol.

title: bridge-whatsapp
input: Connect my WhatsApp to my agent. I want to see and reply to messages from my node instead of opening the app.
hint: Bridge module. contentType=social-bridge, boundary=none. Internal-only despite session mechanics. Bridge translates platform protocol to internal events. PM routes everything. Use controller protocol.

title: bridge-unified-inbox
input: I use Telegram for my hobby group and WhatsApp for family. Show me both in one inbox but keep them visually separated.
hint: Cross-bridge composition. Unified inbox composing two bridge modules. boundary=none (aggregator of internal bridges). Visual separation via tabs or sections. Use controller protocol.

title: bridge-cross-post
input: I want to post the same update to Bluesky, Telegram, and X simultaneously from one compose view.
hint: Outbound fan-out. PM routes to one native A2A module (Bluesky) and two bridges (Telegram, X). Model must handle protocol difference. Use controller protocol.

title: bridge-to-booking
input: When someone messages me on X about booking a session, my agent should automatically route that to my booking module.
hint: Bridge-to-module routing. X bridge (S3) → PM intent classification → booking module (S5). PM reads inbound, classifies intent, routes. Use controller protocol.

title: bridge-focus-hold
input: If someone sends me a WhatsApp message while I'm in a focus session, hold it and show me a summary when the session ends.
hint: Behavioral constraint on bridge. bThread blocks message rendering during focus, accumulates, summarizes on release. The bridge does NOT make this decision — PM behavioral engine does. Use controller protocol.

title: bridge-recipe-telegram
input: I've been using my recipe collection privately. Now I want to share new recipes automatically to my Telegram cooking group whenever I add one.
hint: TRICKY: User says 'share' but correct behavior is NOT boundary change. Recipe stays boundary=none (internal-only). PM adds behavioral trigger routing recipe-added events through Telegram bridge. No Agent Card metadata. Use controller protocol.
